title: phux-protocol: wasm-safe input atoms (no libghostty-vt-sys on wasm)
description: The wire input types (KeyEvent's Key/Action/Mods) currently come from libghostty-vt-sys (FFI). The web client must put these VALUES on the wire without linking the zig lib. Provide phux-defined mirror enums (matching libghostty discriminants) gated for the non-server/wasm build, so phux-protocol input encoding compiles to wasm with zero native deps. Server keeps using the libghostty encoder.
close_reason: Done + verified (commit 3ea2fc4). Wire owns its atoms (ADR-0023): PhysicalKey copied enum + native KeyAction/ModSet/Focus/Mouse atoms; input+wire un-gated; libghostty conversions confined to server encoder boundary. phux-protocol codec builds on wasm32; full workspace builds; 919/919 tests pass.

comments.author: phall1
comments.text: SCOPE CLARIFIED: phux-protocol gates BOTH 'pub mod input' AND 'pub mod wire' behind feature='server' (lib.rs:29-34), and server pulls libghostty-vt (FFI). So the default wasm build has NO codec at all. The browser client needs the wire codec on wasm. Plan: expose wire without libghostty by giving the input frames (InputKey/InputMouse -> KeyEvent/MouseEvent) phux-defined wasm-safe atoms (mirror libghostty's Key/Action/Mods discriminants; server converts at its libghostty boundary). RECEIVE path (TerminalOutput/TerminalSnapshot/Attached/Error/Pong) needs NO input atoms — so a receive+render-only client is reachable first, then input. Must not break the 321 server tests.

comments.author: phall1
comments.text: Refactor fully scoped + ADR-0023 committed. Exact atom spec (must match libghostty discriminants for wire compat): KeyAction #[repr(u32)] Release=0,Press=1,Repeat=2. ModSet bitflags u16: SHIFT=1,CTRL=2,ALT=4,SUPER=8,CAPS_LOCK=16,NUM_LOCK=32,SHIFT_SIDE=64,CTRL_SIDE=128,ALT_SIDE=256,SUPER_SIDE=512. PhysicalKey(u32) newtype. Mouse Action Release=0/Press=1, Button Left=2/Right=3/None=0. CONVERSION BOUNDARY is localized: phux-server/src/input/key.rs key_event_to_libghostty does set_action(ev.action).set_key(ev.key).set_mods(ev.mods) -> insert .into()/.try_into(). Same for mouse/focus. Steps: (1) native atoms+From/TryFrom under cfg(server) in phux-protocol input/{key,mouse,focus}.rs; (2) un-gate pub mod input+wire in lib.rs; (3) libghostty-vt optional; (4) .into() at server encoders; (5) verify phux-protocol builds wasm w/ codec + 321 server tests green.

title: phux-web crate: rust->wasm client (wasm-bindgen/web-sys, canvas, WS, input)
description: New crate phux-web compiled to wasm: browser WebSocket transport (gloo-net) speaking phux-protocol; demux TERMINAL_OUTPUT into per-pane libghostty-vt cores via phux-client-core; canvas render bridge (web-sys) driven by libghostty RenderState dirty rows; browser keyboard/mouse -> INPUT_* wire frames; viewport/terminal resize. Thin shell over phux-client-core.
close_reason: Done + VERIFIED end-to-end in a real browser (daabea2). Headless Chrome: wasm client connects to a live phux server over WebSocket, HELLO+ATTACH, decodes SNAPSHOT/OUTPUT with the real phux-protocol codec, feeds ghostty-vt.wasm, renders the seed marker to canvas; keyboard->INPUT_KEY wired. Also node-tested (session) + native-e2e (live attach).

title: phux-server: Transport trait + WebSocket transport
description: Abstract the UDS accept loop (runtime.rs accept_loop/handle_client are hardcoded to UnixListener/UnixStream) behind a Transport trait over AsyncRead+AsyncWrite, then add a WebSocket transport (tokio-tungstenite) carrying the identical FrameKind frames. The web client connects here. Reuse for satellites later.
close_reason: Landed in 3451139: frame-level Transport abstraction (FrameReader/FrameWriter/Incoming), WS listener via tokio-tungstenite, opt-in PHUX_WS_ADDR. 321 tests pass incl. new WS ping/pong round-trip; clippy -D warnings clean.

title: Extract phux-client-core (the no-refactor seam)
description: Extract the platform-agnostic client logic from phux-client into a new crate phux-client-core: per-pane libghostty Terminal mirror (driver.rs PaneSlot), binary-split layout tree (layout.rs/multi_pane.rs), input->wire encoding, output routing, focus. NO I/O, no ratatui, no stdout. Refactor the native TUI to a thin shell over it (ratatui chrome + termios stays in phux-client). This is what lets native and web share logic with zero divergence.
close_reason: DESCOPED. The web client (phux-web) is intentionally a lean, independent wire consumer (single terminal, canvas render) — its rendering and concerns differ entirely from the native multi-pane ratatui TUI, so a shared phux-client-core would de-duplicate little while coupling two very different consumers. The webasm stack works end-to-end without it. A future native+web shared core can be revisited if real duplication emerges; not required for the wasm client.

title: ghostty-vt.wasm rendering core + web-sys Rust driver
description: REFRAMED (no longer make-or-break): ghostty ships ghostty-vt as a STANDALONE wasm module (build.zig initWasm: addExecutable, rdynamic, no-entry, 'for embedders e.g. JS in a browser') — NOT a linkable archive. And libghostty-vt is FFI-deep (key.rs -> ffi::ghostty_key_encoder_*; lib.rs re-exports libghostty_vt_sys). DECISION: do NOT link zig into the Rust wasm binary. Build ghostty-vt.wasm from local ghostty (zig initWasm path), and write a Rust binding that loads+drives it via web-sys WebAssembly (Terminal::new/write/cells/dirty/resize). The Rust client stays pure Rust; ghostty-vt is a separate module. Eliminates the rust+zig shared-memory problem entirely.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p libghostty-vt --target wasm32-unknown-unknown succeeds; a smoke test feeds VT bytes to a Terminal and reads back grid state in wasm.
close_reason: Done + verified (commits 6ce499c, 32e8753). phux-vt-web crate: Vt::load instantiates ghostty-vt.wasm; Terminal::{write,resize,rows_text,grid} drive it + read the styled grid (fg/bg/defaults). wasm-pack --node: reads text back, truecolor cell. Canvas render verified in headless Chrome (1704dd4).

comments.author: phall1
comments.text: VERIFIED engine drive (node, real ghostty-vt.wasm): built ghostty-vt.wasm from local ghostty via 'zig build -Demit-lib-vt -Dtarget=wasm32-freestanding'. 170 exports = full libghostty-vt C ABI; ONLY import is env.log (self-contained, own allocator via ghostty_wasm_alloc_*). Node probe PASSES: ghostty_terminal_new(0,out,opts_ptr)->0, resize->0, vt_write ok, render_state_new->0, render_state_update->0. Key ABI facts: by-value TerminalOptions lowers to a pointer (wasm32 layout: cols u16@0, rows u16@2, max_scrollback u32@4, size 8); null allocator valid. Verification paths confirmed: node for engine, wasm-pack --node for the Rust driver, Chrome 148 installed for browser e2e. NEXT: cell/grid read (row+cell iterators), then the Rust web-sys driver.

comments.author: phall1
comments.text: Rust driver landed + VERIFIED (commit 6ce499c). wasm-pack test --node: Vt::load instantiates ghostty-vt.wasm, Terminal::{write,resize,rows_text} drive it; reads 'Hello, phux' back from the grid and 'world\r'+'W'->'World'. Caught/fixed a real bug (cells-container pointer must be rebound in-place per row, like the row iterator). REMAINING for full render: per-cell fg/bg colors (FG_COLOR=6/BG_COLOR=5 tags, same read pattern), default colors (render_state COLOR_FOREGROUND/BACKGROUND), cursor pos; then the canvas renderer. NEXT: colors + grid() API, then canvas + Trunk + headless-Chrome render test.

title: phux-protocol: guarantee + CI-gate wasm32 build
description: Lock in that phux-protocol (default features = the wire codec) compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown and keep it that way. Default deps (thiserror/bytes/bitflags) are wasm-clean. The libghostty-vt-backed input atoms (key.rs) are behind the optional 'server' feature; decide the web client's feature set (it needs the key atoms, which depend on T2). Add a CI job building the crate for wasm32.
close_reason: Done: phux-protocol (default features) compiles to wasm32 WITH the FrameKind codec after phux-486.8 un-gated wire/input. Verified via cargo build -p phux-protocol --target wasm32-unknown-unknown.

title: phux-web: Rust/WASM browser client speaking the wire
description: A browser client for phux written in Rust->WASM, sharing the real wire codec (phux-protocol) and the real terminal engine (libghostty-vt on wasm32) with native phux — not a JS reimplementation. Proves the thesis (ADR-0017): the browser is another consumer of the wire, alongside the TUI and agents. Architected so native and web both sit on a platform-agnostic phux-client-core, so no re-architecture is needed later.
design: Seams: (1) phux-protocol reused as-is (wasm-clean). (2) libghostty-vt compiled to wasm32 — same engine as native. (3) NEW phux-client-core: platform-agnostic (per-pane libghostty mirror, binary-split layout tree, input->wire encoding, output routing; no I/O, no ratatui, no stdout). (4) native phux-client and phux-web become thin shells over the core. (5) phux-server gains a Transport trait + WebSocket impl (same FrameKind codec). (6) phux-web: wasm-bindgen/web-sys, canvas render, gloo-net WS, browser input; built with Trunk, minimal JS. phux-site embeds the Trunk artifact.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE. Browser client speaking the wire, verified end-to-end in headless Chrome: live phux server -> WebSocket -> real phux-protocol codec (wasm) -> ghostty-vt.wasm engine -> canvas render, keyboard->INPUT_KEY. All 8 children done+verified. Native workspace 919 tests + clippy/fmt/docs gates green; wasm driver/session/render/e2e tests green.

title: phux snapshot <target> --json: structured screen read (agent floor)
description: The floor of ADR-0022. A thin one-shot CLI: connect to the per-user server over UDS (auto-spawn like attach), ATTACH to the target session, replay the authoritative TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT into a libghostty Terminal, walk the grid, emit structured JSON {schema_version, pane, cols, rows, cursor:{x,y,visible}, lines:[{text}]}, detach, exit. Default = text lines + cursor + dims; --cells adds per-cell {ch,fg,bg,attrs,semantic} later. This replaces the bolted-on libghostty oracle (pty_screen example) with a real phux capability and reads the server's authoritative grid (no PTY/tmux). Bootstrap impl may walk client-side (ADR-0022 §5 permits); the CLI contract is stable regardless. Establish the JSON schema + schema_version here.
notes: Bootstrap landed (attach-walk). phux snapshot <session> --json emits ScreenState v1 {schema_version,pane,cols,rows,cursor,lines}; crates/phux-client/src/snapshot.rs + phux/src/main.rs. Dogfooded against a seeded server. REMAINING for 'done right': the read-only server-side GET_SCREEN query (walk the server's own Terminal, no attach/resize) so snapshot has no side effect; and --cells (per-cell style + OSC-133 semantic marks). The CLI contract is stable; server-side is a swap-under.
close_reason: snapshot 'done right': side-effect-free GET_SCREEN server query (Command tag 0x07) replaces the attach-walk; ScreenState moved to phux-core; phux snapshot rewired. Verified empirically (server+send-keys+snapshot, no tmux) and via unit+integration+wire-roundtrip tests.

title: [epic] phux as a tool for agents — structured CLI + event surface (ADR-0022)
description: Make phux the terminal-capability substrate agents use (ADR-0022). One server core, many projections; CLI + JSON schema as the stable contract; wire additive/versioned. Phased: floor (snapshot/send/ls --json) -> semantics (cells + run + events) -> adapters+dogfooding (MCP, examples/agents, skill). See ADR-0022.

title: Splitting a pane blanks the original (source) pane's content
description: Repro (tmux-free, via crates/phux/examples/pty_screen.rs): attach, type 'echo MARKER' so the pane has content, then split (C-a | OR C-a -). The NEW pane shows a fresh shell prompt correctly, but the ORIGINAL pane you split from goes completely BLANK — its prior content (MARKER + prompt) disappears. Happens for both vertical (| , cols shrink) and horizontal (-, rows shrink) splits, so it is not cols-shrink-specific. PRE-EXISTING: reproduced on commit 706fc41 (before phux-tnh), so the phux-tnh pane-close reflow fix did not introduce it. Same family as phux-8v1 (libghostty reflow content-loss on the resized mirror + a state-sync gap), BUT notably phux-tnh's TerminalSpawned resync (which now emits TERMINAL_RESIZE for the shrunk original leaf on split) does NOT restore the content — so there is an additional mechanism beyond the resize resync: either the original pane's server Terminal loses content on its split reflow (so the resync snapshot is itself blank), or the split's SPAWN racing the original-pane resize leaves the resync capturing an empty grid. ONLY visible without tmux — tmux retains/reflows the byte stream and masks it. Verification rig: crates/phux/examples/pty_screen.rs (raw PTY + libghostty oracle, NO tmux). Investigate the local split action path (input_dispatch/actions split-pane), the original pane's client mirror reflow in paint_full_frame, and whether/what the server resyncs for the original leaf on split.
notes: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed tmux-free via pty_screen + focus-back experiment): the original pane's content is NOT lost permanently. Chain: (1) on split, paint_full_frame's safe_resize reflows the original pane's CLIENT mirror, which is lossy on shrink (same libghostty reflow issue as phux-8v1) so render_at_full paints it blank; (2) the server resync (phux-8v1/phux-tnh, ~50ms later) restores the mirror content via TERMINAL_OUTPUT vt_write, BUT non-focused panes are never repainted (server_frame.rs TerminalOutput/TerminalSnapshot handlers render only the FOCUSED pane), so the restored content is invisible; (3) focusing the pane (C-a h) triggers paint_full_frame which renders it via paint_focused_pane -> content reappears. PROOF: 'echo LEFTMARK999; C-a |; C-a h' shows LEFTMARK999 in the left pane. BIGGER BUG EXPOSED: non-focused panes are visually FROZEN — output to an unfocused pane updates its mirror but never repaints to screen until focus/full-repaint. FIX: repaint non-focused panes (cheap dirty-tracked render_at at their rect) when they receive TERMINAL_OUTPUT/SNAPSHOT, restoring the focused pane's cursor afterward. This fixes both the split-blank and the frozen-non-focused-pane class.
close_reason: Fixed in edb3c17. Root cause: non-focused panes were never repainted on TERMINAL_OUTPUT/SNAPSHOT (only the focused pane rendered), so the post-split resync that restores the original pane's mirror content was invisible until focus-switch. Fix: TERMINAL_OUTPUT handler now repaints non-focused panes at their rect (dirty-tracked render_at) + restores focused cursor. Also fixes the general frozen-non-focused-pane bug. Verified tmux-free via pty_screen for both | and - splits.

title: Client/server crash on terminal resize: libghostty PageList.resizeCols integer overflow when cols AND rows both shrink in one resize() call
description:
REPRO (verified via isolated tmux + resize-window): start phux attach at 100x30, resize terminal smaller in BOTH dimensions (e.g. 60x20) -> phux client panics with 'panic: integer overflow' inside libghostty Zig: PageList.resizeCols <- PageList.resize <- Terminal.resize <- ghostty_terminal_resize, called from phux_client::attach::paint::paint_full_frame (driver.rs:734 SIGWINCH branch). Process dies.

MATRIX (single pane, from 100x30):
- shrink cols only (->60x30): OK
- shrink rows only (->100x20): OK
- grow (->larger): OK
- shrink cols AND rows together (->60x20): CRASH
Splits crash the same way; not split-specific.

ROOT CAUSE: libghostty's PageList.resizeCols overflows when both dimensions decrease in a single resize() call (Zig integer-overflow safety check; debug build). phux depends on libghostty-vt as a pinned git rev, so the upstream Zig fix is out of this repo.

SERVER EXPOSURE: the same both-shrink hits the server too — on SIGWINCH the client sends TerminalResize(cols,rows) and terminal_actor.rs:1022 calls terminal.resize(cols,rows,...). A both-shrink there crashes the server.

FIX (phux-side workaround): a safe_resize(terminal, cols, rows) that, when both axes shrink vs the Terminal's current cols()/rows(), decomposes into two single-axis resize() calls (shrink rows first, then cols) — each individually safe. Apply at all libghostty resize call sites: client paint.rs (x2), client server_frame.rs:161, server terminal_actor.rs:1022. Long-term: fix/upstream PageList.resizeCols and drop the workaround.
close_reason: Fixed in 4afee05 via safe_resize decomposition (client paint.rs + server terminal_actor.rs): a both-axis shrink is split into two single-axis resize() calls, each safe. Verified single-pane + split both-shrink no longer crashes and redraws cleanly. Root cause is upstream ghostty PageList.resizeCols (self.rows - c.y - 1 underflow); fixing there separately. Workaround stays since phux pins a libghostty-vt git rev.

title: Auto-spawned server not daemonized: shares client controlling terminal, dies when terminal closes -> sessions vanish
notes: FIXED + verified. maybe_auto_spawn_server (crates/phux/src/main.rs) spawned the server as a plain child sharing the client's process group + controlling terminal (code even admitted 'good enough for demo'). Closing that terminal SIGHUP'd the server -> sessions vanished. FIX: auto-spawn now passes a hidden --daemonize flag; run_server calls rustix::process::setsid() before binding so the server lands in its own session with no controlling terminal (verified ps: PGID==PID, SESS=0). stderr redirected to phux-server.log beside the socket for debuggability. Added rustix 'process' feature to phux/Cargo.toml (same crate already used by phux-client; no new workspace dep). Verified: destroyed the originating terminal while attached -> server SURVIVED, session reattachable from a fresh terminal with content intact. Note: the zombie-client fix [[phux-bln]] also independently helped, but daemonization is the robust decoupling.

title: Detach never exits client: tokio::io::stdin reader thread hangs runtime teardown -> zombie steals first post-detach line (reattach 'does nothing')
notes: FIXED + verified. Root cause: detach returned Ok(()) and relied on unwinding, but tokio::io::stdin() (driver.rs) parks an UNCANCELLABLE blocking read on a helper thread; the RawModeGuard Drop restored termios fine, but the subsequent tokio runtime drop blocked forever waiting for that stuck read(). Result: a zombie client (confirmed alive 3s+ after detach) that kept a reader on the shared PTY and stole the FIRST line the user typed next -- typically the reattach command, so reattach appeared to do nothing. FIX: exit_after_detach() in driver.rs restores the terminal (mirrors terminal_reset_on_signal) and process::exit(0) at all three outcome.exit / detach-disconnect sites, bypassing the hung teardown. Verified via tmux harness: client gone <1s after detach, first post-detach command executes, reattach from the SAME shell works.

title: Split/resize leaves the surviving pane blank: paint_full_frame clears screen but render_at honors incremental dirty tracking
notes: paint_full_frame emits ED2 (\x1b[2J) then re-renders each pane via render_at. render_at early-returns on Dirty::Clean and only paints per-row-dirty rows. The pane that SURVIVES a split/resize has unchanged grid content, so libghostty reports it Clean -> render_at paints nothing -> the just-cleared screen leaves it BLANK. (Symptom looked like 'old pane shows stale content' but that stale content was actually the NEW pane's own fresh-shell zsh-init output; the original pane was simply empty.) FIX: added TerminalRenderer::render_at_full (forces Dirty::Full) and a force_full param on paint_focused_pane; paint_full_frame now forces a full redraw of every pane while the hot incremental TERMINAL_OUTPUT path stays dirty-tracked. Verified via tmux harness: SHORT-01..05 survive a '|' split into the left 40-col pane.
close_reason: Fixed: render_at_full + force_full on paint_focused_pane (committed b98a2c1, render.rs:174/paint.rs). paint_full_frame forces full redraw of every pane; hot incremental path stays dirty-tracked. Verified via tmux harness.

title: Split direction inverted: '|' produced stacked panes (horizontal divider) instead of side-by-side
notes: split_dir_arg in crates/phux-client/src/attach/input_dispatch.rs mapped the config 'direction' string straight to the same-named SplitDir variant. But the config string names the DIVIDER orientation (tmux model, documented in default.toml: '|' = vertical divider = side-by-side panes) while SplitDir names the SPLIT AXIS (Horizontal = splits width = side-by-side; Vertical = splits height = stacked, per layout::fill_rects). So 'vertical' was mapping to SplitDir::Vertical (stacked) when it should be SplitDir::Horizontal (side-by-side). FIX: crossed the mapping (horizontal->Vertical, vertical->Horizontal) + updated the unit test. Verified via scripts/tui-probe.sh: '|' now yields a vertical divider with side-by-side panes.
close_reason: Fixed: split_dir_arg mapping crossed in input_dispatch.rs:582-583 ('horizontal' divider->SplitDir::Vertical, 'vertical' divider->SplitDir::Horizontal) + unit test updated. '|' now yields side-by-side panes.

title: Live phux session: leader key inert, can't split, status bar shows only time, cursor stranded bottom-right
description:
Reported live by phall1 on main @ 2460eeb (2026-05-27 evening).

## Symptoms

- Cursor parks at bottom-right of host terminal for the entire session.
- Status bar shows only the time widget on the right; left (session-name) is blank.
- Prefix key (C-a) does nothing.
- Cannot split (C-a |, C-a -, all inert).

## Hypothesis (composite or single root?)

Two plausible root causes — possibly the same one, possibly two:

1. **Config path issue**: if phux_config::loader::load() degraded silently, both the resolver and status bar are disabled. Counter-evidence: time widget renders, so SOME status config is built — would expect a totally blank bar if config failed. Maybe only the session-name widget is failing (empty session_name string), in which case the resolver should still work.

2. **focused_pane is None when paint_full_frame runs**: see crates/phux-client/src/attach/paint.rs:100-117. paint_bar_after_pane is called with fallback_origin=None, and the focused-pane render is skipped when focused is None. The cursor stays at the status bar's tail (bottom-right). This explains the cursor symptom, but not the leader-key symptom unless the resolver path is also gated on focused_pane being Some.

3. **Input not reaching resolver**: c0_or_ascii_to_key(0x01) does emit KeyEvent { mods: CTRL, key: A }, and the resolver build was verified to produce Feed::Partial → Feed::Resolved("split-pane") for "C-a |". So the resolver itself is fine. Either the parser path is not feeding bytes, or consume_chord is not being called.

## Repro capture needed

User to run:
  RUST_LOG=trace,phux_client=trace ./target/debug/phux attach 2>/tmp/phux.log
  # press C-a once
  # press |
  # press C-a d to detach
Then attach /tmp/phux.log to this issue.

## Refs

- Last code commit: 034f37e (fix clippy in input dispatch)
- Default config: crates/phux-config/src/default.toml (prefix=C-a, prefix-table |/-/h/j/k/l/x/n/p/d, [status] left=[session-name], right=[time])
- Resolver verified via smoke test: produces Partial then Resolved("split-pane") for C-a |.
- Paint cursor restoration: phux-9xn, phux-dca, phux-b9n landed restore-on-bar-repaint.

LABELS: area:client, area:input, area:tui, crate:phux-client, regression
notes: REOPENED 2026-05-28: prior 'fix' (CUP-restore band-aids in paint.rs) never worked live. Real root cause: every paint path computes the correct cursor-restore CUP but NEVER FLUSHES it. stdout is a LineWriter; the restore CUP has no trailing newline, and dropping the StdoutLock guard does not flush the global buffer. render_at() flushes mid-frame BEFORE the restore CUP is written; the bar paint + restore CUP that follow sit in the buffer until the next pane output. At an idle prompt no output arrives, so the host cursor strands at the bar's tail = bottom-right. The phux-gxy unit tests passed because they assert on the in-memory Vec buffer, which never exercises the flush. FIX: flush stdout after the final cursor write in paint_bar_after_pane() and paint_full_frame(). Verified empirically via new scripts/tui-probe.sh harness: cursor now lands at the shell prompt (2,22) instead of (80,23).
close_reason: Resolved by commits since the 2460eeb report. Verified end-to-end via scripts/tui-probe.sh (80x24): C-a | splits into side-by-side panes (leader live, k2a direction correct), both panes paint (d69 force-full redraw), cursor lands in the new right pane's prompt at 43,22 (NOT stranded bottom-right 79,23), status bar time widget renders. Residual: status-bar LEFT (session-name) still blank — that is phux-17u (session-name not wired through ATTACHED), tracked separately.

title: Charter: docs/CONVENTIONS.md (the meta-doc)
description: Write docs/CONVENTIONS.md: the single artifact that defines the new doc system. Specifies the 3-layer model, frontmatter schema (audience: humans|agents|consumers|contributors, stability: stable|evolving|scratch, last-reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD), the 50-word TL;DR rule, where each kind of content goes, and the ADR template + Status: controlled vocabulary. This is the artifact every other restructure ticket points at.

Defines: 3-layer model, frontmatter schema (audience/stability/last-reviewed), 50-word TL;DR rule, where-new-content-goes flowchart, ADR template + Status controlled vocabulary (Accepted | Accepted (forward-compat) | Superseded by NNNN | Deprecated), style rules, CI gate list, research/scratch policy.

title: BUG: vim renders only half the screen — pane area smaller than host viewport
description:
User-reported: opening vim under phux shows the buffer occupying roughly half the host terminal's vertical space.

## Possible root causes (need investigation)
1. **Server PTY sized smaller than host viewport.** ATTACH carries `viewport: ViewportInfo` (driver.rs:300) but the server may apply only a default 80x24 to the spawned PTY on first attach, ignoring the ATTACH viewport. Confirm by reading the server-side ATTACH handler in crates/phux-server/ and the spawn-on-attach path.

2. **Pane area computed at half-viewport on the CLIENT.** If `multi_pane::compute_layout` returns a Rect with h=viewport.rows/2 for the single-pane case, the client-side libghostty mirror would be resized smaller than the server's PTY, so visible cells are only the top half. Check default LayoutState single-pane case + compute_layout in multi_pane.rs.

3. **Stale split.** User may have accidentally triggered a vertical/horizontal split via the new `|` or `-` chord (phux-9fu) and there's a hidden invisible second pane taking half. Check by attaching and inspecting layout state; if there are 2 leaves with one of dim 0 or one blank, the split-on-action path has a bug.

4. **Status bar reserves the wrong number of rows.** pane_viewport reserves 1 row (paint.rs:151-157). If it reserves N rows incorrectly, vim sees a shrunken viewport. Unlikely (subtraction is just 1) but worth checking when status bar wraps.

## Reproduction
`phux attach` then `vim` — observe buffer height vs host terminal height.

## Acceptance
- vim opens at the full available height (host_rows - 1 for status bar).
- The server's PTY for the focused pane matches the client's pane Rect dimensions.
- No invisible second pane in default single-pane attach.

References:
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs:298-313 (send_attach)
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/multi_pane.rs (compute_layout)
- crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs (ATTACH handler)
- ADR-0013 (wire is VT bytes — server PTY size IS the canonical size)

Root cause: server's handle_attach in runtime.rs had _viewport prefixed with underscore — the ATTACH viewport was literally dropped on the floor. Panes spawned at hardcoded 80x24 and stayed there until first SIGWINCH. Fix: un-underscore + new apply_attach_viewport helper that mirrors handle_viewport_resize's per-actor resize fan-out. New regression test attach_viewport_resize.rs uses 'stty size' loop in PTY to verify post-ATTACH winsize; stash-verified to catch the bug (88 consecutive '24 80' readings without the fix).

title: BUG: cursor pinned at bottom-right of screen after fresh attach (paint sequence strands cursor when last_cursor is None)
description:
User-reported regression observed after phux-5ke + phux-b9n landed.

## Symptom
Cursor sits at the bottom-right corner of the host terminal after `phux attach`. Does not move with the focused pane's cursor.

## Hypothesised root cause (paint.rs:140-144)
`paint_bar_after_pane` only restores the cursor when `restore_cursor: Option<(u16,u16)>` is `Some`. `restore_cursor` traces back to `slot.renderer.last_cursor()` which returns `None` whenever libghostty's `Snapshot::cursor_viewport()` returns None — which happens on fresh attach (no PTY output yet), during alt-screen transitions, and any frame where the cursor sits outside the viewport.

In those None cases, the previous `StatusBarPainter::paint` writes to the bottom-row cells and naturally leaves the terminal cursor at the end of the bar (bottom-right). The phux-b9n fix made the cursor visible but did not give it a fallback landing position.

## Fix sketch
When `restore_cursor` is None, fall back to positioning at the focused pane's origin + (0,0) (or last known position). Two reasonable options:
1. Cache the last non-None cursor per slot; reuse when current is None.
2. Position cursor at focused pane's (rect.x, rect.y) — guaranteed to be inside the pane and away from the bar.

Option 2 is simpler and matches user expectation (cursor inside the visible pane).

## Acceptance
- `phux attach` shows the cursor inside the focused pane immediately, not at bottom-right.
- vim, less, htop, nvim cursor positioning still correct (they emit their own CUP).
- Regression test: extend an existing paint test or add a new one asserting cursor position is inside pane rect after `paint_full_frame` with a freshly-created pane (no PTY output).

References:
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/paint.rs:116-145 (paint_bar_after_pane)
- crates/phux-client/src/attach/render.rs:73-74 (last_cursor doc)
- commit 138c772 (phux-b9n cursor-invisible fix)
close_reason: Landed on main as 78b316c (cherry-picked from fanout/phux-9xn-dca-cursor-bar). paint_bar_after_pane now plumbs the focused pane's Rect origin as a fallback; when last_cursor is None it emits CSI {y+1};{x+1}H + ESC[?25l so the cursor lands inside the pane area (hidden) rather than stranded at bottom-right after the bar paint. 3 new regression tests in attach/paint.rs::tests. 264 phux-client tests + integrated workspace 786/786 green.

title: Cursor invisible on fresh attach (status bar ?25l strands cursor)
description:
Surfaced immediately after phux-5ke landed on main (2026-05-27).
User reports cursor not visible on `phux attach`.

## Root cause

`render::chrome::status_bar::write_buffer` emitted `\x1b[?25l` (hide
cursor) at the start of every bar paint with the comment "Cursor stays
hidden — caller restores at the focused pane." The caller
(`attach::paint::paint_bar_after_pane`) only restored visibility when
`restore_cursor.is_some()`.

`restore_cursor` traces to `slot.renderer.last_cursor()`, which is
None whenever `libghostty_vt::Snapshot::cursor_viewport()` returns None
(per libghostty docs: "if it is currently visible within the viewport").
On a fresh attach with no PTY output yet, libghostty has no viewport
cursor and returns None — so the bar's `?25l` strands the cursor
invisible until the first pane render with a visible cursor.

Pre-existed wave-2 (the OLD hand-painted status_bar.rs also emitted
`?25l`), but went unnoticed because the older paint paths happened to
re-emit `?25h` more often. Wave-2's collapsed paint sequence
(`paint_full_frame` calls bar LAST with no following pane render) makes
the bug consistently visible on fresh attach.

## Fix landed

`crates/phux-client/src/render/chrome/status_bar.rs::write_buffer` no
longer emits `\x1b[?25l`. The flicker concern from the original comment
is sub-millisecond on modern terminals; the bug it created was visible
on every fresh attach.

`crates/phux-client/src/attach/paint.rs::paint_full_frame` reordered:
focused pane now paints LAST (after dividers and status bar) so its
`render_at` is the final cursor-positioning + DECTCEM emit. This
matches the comment in `render::chrome::dividers` that already claimed
"the focused-pane render runs after dividers."

Incremental path (`server_frame::handle_server_frame`) unchanged —
still emits `?25h` via `paint_bar_after_pane`'s restore branch when
`focused_cursor.is_some()`.

## Acceptance

- `phux attach` shows the cursor immediately, before any PTY output.
- `just ci` green (721 tests).
- No regression in vim/less cursor-hide behavior (focused pane render
  still owns DECTCEM via `snapshot.cursor_visible()`).
notes: Landed in #31 (138c772).

title: [epic] Lazy state synchronization wire — server-side implementation per ADR-0018
description:
Implement ADR-0018 (Lazy state synchronization). Wire shape is unchanged; semantics of TERMINAL_OUTPUT.bytes shift from "PTY pass-through" to "minimum VT to transition consumer's last-acked state to canonical's current state".

Scope per ADR-0018 addendum (2026-05-26 revision): the per-consumer cache primitive is libghostty RenderState, already used in-tree. The work is lifecycle (per-attached-consumer RenderState ownership in TerminalActor) + incremental synthesis (extend SnapshotSynthesizer) + tick scheduler + ack-driven dirty reset. Estimated ~200-400 LOC of new code plus per-consumer cache management.

Blocked on phux-28f (TerminalActor placement) for the lifecycle piece. The synthesis extension can proceed in parallel against the existing SnapshotSynthesizer.

References:
- ADR-0018 (Lazy state synchronization) + 2026-05-26 addendum
- research/2026-05-26-state-sync-algorithm.md (revised Dependencies §)
- bd memory: renderstate-as-cache, mosh-decomposition (updated)
close_reason: Core state-sync stack shipped: q0e.1 (incremental synthesizer #4), q0e.2 (per-consumer RenderState lifecycle #3), q0e.3 (tick scheduler #6), q0e.4 (FRAME_ACK handler closing the loop #7). Plus phux-l0t (libghostty FFI bug diagnosis #5) and phux-6jb (tracking ticket for upstream fix revisit). Only q0e.5 (RTT-adaptive tick) remains, deferred per its description. ADR-0018 fully implemented end-to-end.

title: client: attach failure wedges terminal (alt-screen + cursor hidden); graceful failure UX
description:
Repro: \`phux attach <session>\` when no server is running, or when the session doesn't exist, or when the client gets signal-killed mid-attach. Terminal is left in alt-screen mode + cursor hidden + sometimes raw mode. User has to type \`reset\` blind.

Root causes:
1. \`attach::run\` installs RawModeGuard (alt-screen + raw mode + hide cursor) BEFORE the UDS connect + handshake. Any pre-handshake failure flickers the terminal through alt-screen for nothing.
2. RawModeGuard::Drop only runs on Rust unwinding. SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGHUP bypass it. The cleanup escapes (\\x1b[?1049l + \\x1b[?25h) never get written.
3. No signal handler installed to restore terminal state on signal exit.
4. Auto-spawn server always uses DEFAULT_SESSION_NAME ('default') but client asks for whatever was on the CLI. Mismatch causes attach to fail at handshake while client is already in alt-screen.
5. Connection errors surface as 'attach failed: connection refused' — actionable would be 'no phux server at <path>; start one with \`phux server\`' with the exact suggested command.
6. Stale socket files survive a crashed server (separate ticket — file follow-up).

Acceptance for THIS ticket:
- attach::run defers RawModeGuard installation until AFTER handshake + ATTACH succeeds (or skip alt-screen entirely on early failure).
- SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers installed that run terminal cleanup before exit.
- ConnectionRefused/NotFound socket errors produce a one-line clean error WITHOUT entering alt-screen.
- Auto-spawn passes the user's requested session name through to the spawned server.
- Test under the new \`Screen\` harness: a synthetic 'no server' scenario captures stdout and asserts NO alt-screen-enter byte sequence appears.

Out-of-scope: stale-socket cleanup (file follow-up bd if not already covered).

title: feat(server): wire INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_FOCUS dispatch in handle_client
description: handle_client in runtime.rs falls through input frames to the catch-all _ arm, silently dropping every keystroke from attached clients. Wire FrameKind::InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus to the existing PaneActor input mpsc via ServerState. Add a wire-level integration test that drives an InputKey frame through a real UDS connection and verifies it reaches the PaneActor PTY (not just direct PaneActor exercise like pty_pump.rs). Validation: drop with warn! on unknown pane_id or unattached client. Closes the gap noted in phux-byc.6.5 description.
close_reason: Closed

title: byc.8: server-side Terminal placement — spawn_local + LocalSet OR actor pattern
description:
Coming concern for byc.8 (server ATTACH handler). libghostty Terminal is !Send + !Sync (holds NonNull<TerminalImpl>, no unsafe impl Send/Sync in libghostty source).

runtime.rs:258 currently uses tokio::spawn (Send-required) for per-client handler tasks. Today the handler holds no Terminal — only logs unhandled frames. byc.8 will add real ATTACH handling that needs to own a Terminal per pane.

Three viable shapes — byc.8 must pick one:

1. spawn_local + LocalSet — keeps current_thread runtime per ADR-0003; Terminal stays on the runtime thread; preferred.
2. Dedicated pane-supervisor task that owns the Terminal; channels (mpsc) for input/output. The 'actor model' that was flagged earlier in the session as a possible refactor for ServerState shape. Cleaner separation but more plumbing.
3. RefCell<Terminal> behind Rc on a single-threaded executor. Discouraged — fights tokio's spawn conventions.

Do NOT try Arc<Mutex<Terminal>> — won't compile (Mutex requires Send for cross-thread sharing; Terminal isn't Send).

Decision needed at start of byc.8 work. ADR-worthy if the actor pattern is picked.
close_reason: Resolved by ADR-0014 (per-terminal TerminalActor on LocalSet) + TerminalActor impl (crates/phux-server/src/terminal_actor.rs, 987 LOC, uses spawn_local + LocalSet per design 3 in original ticket). The ticket was filed when the actor pattern was still being chosen; ADR-0014 picked option 1 (spawn_local + LocalSet, one borrower, no RefCell) and shipped as TerminalActor in phux-byc.5. Unblocks phux-q0e.2.

title: Per-client capability downsampling: define ColorSupport + downsample fn + per-client storage
description:
SPEC §6.2 conformance: `crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs:121-122` unconditionally
emits `Color::Rgb` with a comment that says "the wire carries the final pixel
value" — directly violating SPEC §6.2: "a client advertising Indexed256 MUST
never receive truecolor RGB cells; the server must downsample."

## Design seam: per-pane capture vs per-client fanout

Three options were considered:

1. First-attach floor — capture in the lowest tier any current client
   advertises. Rejected: locks downsampling into the canonical state; a
   later-attaching TrueColor client cannot recover precision.
2. Per-tier cache — capture once in TrueColor, materialize one cached
   diff per active tier. Rejected as premature; complicates invalidation
   and is unnecessary until profiling shows it.
3. On-demand downsample at fanout — capture once in canonical TrueColor;
   each per-client fanout call downsamples to that client's advertised
   tier. Selected. Matches the existing "server owns canonical state,
   clients see derived views" model and keeps the diff-compute path tier-
   agnostic.

## Scope of THIS ticket (substrate only)

- `ColorSupport` enum (TrueColor | Indexed256 | Indexed16) in phux-protocol.
- `downsample(Color, ColorSupport) -> Color` (as helper fn, since `Color` is a
  re-export of libghostty-vt's StyleColor — no inherent impl possible).
- Per-client cap storage on `AttachedClient` in phux-server::state.
  Defaulted to `ColorSupport::TrueColor` (most-permissive; clients without
  advertised caps don't get downgraded).
- New `phux-server::downsample` module with `downsample_op` /
  `downsample_cell` for byc.5's fanout to plug into.
- Comprehensive unit + property tests.
- `diff_spike` MUST remain byte-identical — downsample is per-fanout, not
  per-capture; nothing in the capture path changes.

## Out of scope (follow-up tickets)

- HELLO ColorSupport wiring (SPEC §6.1 conformance — separate task).
- CIELAB perceptual color distance for downsample (better Indexed16 picks).
- Fanout layer itself (byc.5).
close_reason: Substrate landed in cc30ab5. ColorSupport enum + downsample_color + per-client color_support on AttachedClient + phux-server::downsample helper module + comprehensive tests (unit + proptest). diff_spike byte-identical. Follow-ups: phux-7lf (HELLO wiring), phux-i02 (CIELAB), phux-982 (Cow signature).

title: Wire frames: ATTACH/ATTACHED/DETACH/DETACHED + INPUT_KEY/MOUSE/FOCUS/PASTE
description:
phux-byc.4 surfaced that the message-catalog frames from SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9 are missing from phux-protocol's FrameKind enum. byc.4 landed the server-side state scaffold but its 5 wire-level integration tests had to be deferred until these variants exist. Until this lands, the byc epic cannot demonstrate end-to-end attach. Likely belongs as a phux-6yl follow-up; filing standalone for visibility.

## Required Skills
rust,tlv,protocol-design
design:
phux-protocol::wire::frame::FrameKind currently has: Hello, Ping, PaneDiff.

Add (per SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9):

  C → S
    Attach { session_name: String, role: ?? }   // §7.1
    Detach                                       // §7.3
    InputKey(KeyEvent)                           // §9.1
    InputMouse(MouseEvent)                       // §9.2
    InputFocus(FocusEvent)                       // §9.3
    InputPaste(PasteEvent)                       // §9.4
    InputRaw(Bytes)                              // §9.5 (only if a passthrough is needed; can defer)

  S → C
    Attached { session_id, window_id, pane_id, snapshot: PaneSnapshot }   // §7.2
    Detached                                                              // §7.3
    Bell { pane_id }                                                       // §7.6
    OscEvent { ... }                                                       // §7.7 (defer; not needed for byc.5)
    Alert { ... }                                                          // §7.8 (defer)

Plus the SessionId tagged-union on the wire (per ADR-0007 §3): protocol-level
SessionId is u32 newtype; on-wire it's tagged with the addressed satellite.

For each frame:
  - Add a FrameKind variant
  - Add TLV field IDs in wire/field.rs (per phux-6yl.4)
  - Add encoder + decoder, mirroring how PaneDiff is wired
  - Add an insta hex snapshot covering at least one representative payload
  - Round-trip proptest if the variant has structured payload

phux_protocol::ids::SessionId (wire-side u32) vs phux_core::ids::SessionId
(slotmap key, opaque). They are distinct by design — wire SessionIds are
allocated by the server, slotmap keys are an in-process registry detail.
Add a server-side ::from(phux_core::ids::SessionId) -> wire-side SessionId
mapping + reverse lookup. Don't define a `From` impl in phux-protocol
itself (would force a phux-core dep on phux-protocol).

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol
  cargo run -p phux-server --example diff_spike   # untouched
acceptance_criteria: FrameKind has all the C→S and S→C variants from SPEC §7. Round-trip + insta snapshots cover each new variant. phux-server's deferred byc.4 integration tests now compile (filing a separate ticket for them).
notes: Discovered by phux-byc.4 agent retro. Blocks byc.5 (e2e test), 9gw.3 (attach loop), and the e2e harness epic phux-a87.
close_reason: Landed in fa335e1. Added FrameKind variants Attach/Detach/InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus/InputPaste (C->S) and Attached/Detached/Bell (S->C) per SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9.1-§9.4. Encoders/decoders mirror PaneDiff pattern, 13 insta snapshots + 7 proptest round-trips. Non-obvious decisions for byc.6: (1) AttachRole {Primary=0, Viewer=1} is phux-defined (not in SPEC §13) -- minimum useful split. (2) PaneSnapshot {cols, rows, ops: Vec<DiffOp>} is minimum per SPEC §8.4 -- cursor/modes/scrollback are TODO. (3) Detached carries no body this wave (SPEC §7.3 {reason, message} deferred). (4) FrameKind dropped Eq (now PartialEq only) because MouseEvent has f64. (5) Added DecodeError::UnknownEnumValue {field, value} for libghostty atom round-trip safety. NOT touched: phux-core, phux-server (per task scope), no From<phux_core::ids::SessionId>. Deferred: OscEvent §7.7, Alert §7.8, InputRaw §9.5. All quality gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, nextest (67 protocol + 204 workspace), diff_spike, cargo deny.

title: From impls for all four input event types + server-side KeyEncoder pipeline
description: In phux-server: implement field-for-field conversion from phux_protocol::input::{KeyEvent, MouseEvent, FocusEvent, PasteEvent} to the corresponding libghostty_vt::{key, mouse, focus, paste}::Event types. Wire the key encoder pipeline: per-pane KeyEncoder, set_options_from_terminal(&term) before each encode, encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf). The encoder lives server-side; the wire never carries encoder options (ADR-0006). Mouse/Focus/Paste have their own libghostty encoders that follow the same pattern.
design:
New module: crates/phux-server/src/input/
  key.rs    — impl From<&phux_protocol::input::KeyEvent> for libghostty_vt::key::Event + PerPaneKeyEncoder
  mouse.rs  — impl From for MouseEvent + per-pane mouse encoder
  focus.rs  — impl From for FocusEvent + DEC 1004 gating
  paste.rs  — impl From for PasteEvent + bracketed-paste decision
  mod.rs    — re-exports

PerPaneKeyEncoder holds a libghostty_vt::key::KeyEncoder + reusable Vec<u8>. Calling sites: `encoder.set_options_from_terminal(&term); encoder.encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf)`. Mouse/focus follow the same shape.

Encoder lives server-side. Never serialized to wire. See ADR-0006 rationale.

Conversion is infallible (libghostty event types are supersets / equal-sets of ours).
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: round-trip a KeyEvent through the From impl and assert all fields preserved. Conversion is infallible (no Result).
notes:

title: Server pane plumbing: PTY + per-pane diff loop
description:
Wire a real pane: spawn child PTY, feed bytes into libghostty_vt::Terminal, run grid::capture each frame, compute DiffOps, gate by FRAME_ACK/snapshot fallback per SPEC §6. Pools RenderState/RowIterator/CellIterator per pane (the spike allocates each call). Single-pane, no IPC yet — that's phux-byc.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `cargo run -p phux-server --example one_pane bash` spawns a real shell, accepts typed input from stdin, prints DiffOp summaries to stderr per frame.
- [ ] Steady-state capture loop allocates zero bytes (validated by criterion bench or dhat-rs heap trace).
- [ ] Clean shutdown on child exit (no zombie process, no panic on EOF).
- [ ] diff_spike example continues to print "replay invariant: snap0 + diff(0→1) == snap1   ✓" after the refactor.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-server` green; clippy -D warnings clean.
close_reason: bc1.1 + bc1.2 landed via waves 3+4

title: Protocol foundation: input types + wire codec
description:
Translate SPEC §9 (input) and Appendix A (TLV framing) into Rust types and a length-prefixed codec. Closes the loop between phux_protocol and libghostty_vt by establishing the From<&Event> for libghostty_vt::*::Event boundary, with round-trip snapshot tests. Blocks all wire-format-dependent work (server runtime, client, transport).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A KeyEvent constructed in phux-protocol, converted via the server-side From impl, encoded by libghostty_vt's KeyEncoder, produces VT bytes that match committed insta hex snapshots for ≥10 representative cases (incl. ≥3 KIP enhancement levels).
- [ ] Arbitrary `Vec<DiffOp>` round-trips through the wire codec via proptest (≥1024 cases, no shrinks to failure).
- [ ] Insta hex-dump snapshots committed for ≥8 representative diff frames (small change visible in PR diff).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green; `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean.
- [ ] No libghostty types leak through phux-protocol's public API (verified by `cargo tree -p phux-protocol` not listing libghostty-vt).

title: Flip consumer_tick_emits on: drive client FRAME_ACK + suppress broadcast per tick-managed consumer
description: phux-0q8 wired the per-consumer state-sync LIFECYCLE live (register on ATTACH, free on DETACH) but gated tick emission OFF behind consumer_tick_emits (default false). Enabling the actual lazy-state-sync emission (ADR-0018) requires two things first, or the consumer double-paints: (1) the phux-client must drive the FRAME_ACK loop (it sends none today), else the unacked delta is re-diffed forever; (2) the live broadcast pump (runtime.rs handle_attach) must be SUPPRESSED per tick-managed consumer so exactly one emitter serves each consumer (else two independent seq streams violate SPEC 12.2 monotonic-per-consumer). Then flip the flag and confirm via the existing tests/phux_0q8_no_double_emit.rs (which currently asserts exactly-once under the gated-off path) plus an acked-incremental path test.

title: Surviving pane does not reflow to full viewport after sibling pane closes
description: Repro: in an attached phux session, split a pane (e.g. horizontal split via prefix C-a), then quit/exit the shell in one of the two panes. The surviving pane stays at its small split size instead of growing to fill the freed space. Expected: when a pane closes and the layout collapses to a single pane, the survivor should be resized to the full viewport (its Terminal + PTY winsize grown) and repainted. Symptom suggests the client never sends a resize for the survivor's new larger dims after kill_pane collapses the tree, so the survivor's PTY/Terminal stays at the split dimensions. Related render path: crates/phux-client/src/attach/{driver.rs (TerminalClosed handling), layout.rs (kill_pane), multi_pane.rs (compute_layout), reflow.rs}. Reported live by user 2026-05-29. Verify via scripts/tui-probe.sh: split, exit one pane, assert survivor content fills full height/width.
close_reason: Fixed in e4d0d43. Root cause: on pane close (TERMINAL_CLOSED/EOF) the client folded the layout + resized its local mirror but never sent a TERMINAL_RESIZE for the survivor's grown dims, so the survivor's server PTY stayed at the split size and the shell kept rendering small. Fix: FrameOutcome.reflow_panes (set by TerminalClosed/TerminalSpawned arms) -> driver diffs folded layout vs pre-frame rects and emits per-leaf TERMINAL_RESIZE (same path as SIGWINCH). Verified: split 80x40 -> survivor stty size 19 80; after exit -> 39 80. Also fixes latent split-side sibling-shrink gap.

title: q0e per-consumer state-sync is implemented but not wired into runtime
description: The ADR-0018 lazy state-sync machinery in TerminalActor (consumer_states, register_consumer, tick_emit, synthesize_incremental, ConsumerSyncState, FRAME_ACK handling) is fully implemented but DEAD: runtime.rs constructs the consumer_attach channel with the receiver dropped (let (consumer_attach_tx, _consumer_attach_rx), ~runtime.rs:2512 and 2616), so register_consumer is never invoked and consumer_states is always empty. The live output path is instead the raw PTY-byte broadcast (output_tx) fanned out by per-attach output pumps in runtime.rs (~1548). Net effect: incremental state-sync is not active in production despite phux-q0e being closed; the FRAME_ACK loop is dormant; clients receive raw PTY bytes + full snapshots (attach, and post phux-8v1, live resize) only. Discovered while fixing phux-8v1. Decide: (a) wire register_consumer/tick_emit into handle_attach and switch the output path to the per-consumer tick, or (b) if the raw broadcast is the intended v0.1 path, mark the q0e tick code dead/experimental and update ADR-0018 status + the q0e close note to reflect that it is staged-but-not-active.
close_reason: Lifecycle wired live (register/detach per-consumer RenderState), tick emission gated off (consumer_tick_emits=false) to avoid double-emit vs the live broadcast pump since v0.1 client sends no FRAME_ACK. Regression-locked by tests/phux_0q8_no_double_emit.rs (exactly-once). Production switch deferred to the new follow-up. main cbc7c30, just ci green.

title: Repeating/duplicated characters on real-terminal resize (redraw artifact, post-resize repaint)
description:
REPORTED by phall1 (live, real terminal): resizing the actual host terminal phux runs in leaves repeating/duplicated characters on screen. Distinct from phux-y06 (the resize CRASH, now fixed) — this is a redraw/repaint artifact that was previously masked by the crash and is now visible since resize survives.

KNOWN: SIGWINCH path is crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs:~700-742 -> paint_full_frame (paint.rs). paint_full_frame writes ED2 (\x1b[2J\x1b[H) then re-renders each pane via render_at_full + dividers + bar. Reflow on resize (libghostty) may duplicate lines, or the host terminal's own reflow of pre-repaint content duplicates before phux paints, or scrollback/vt_replay double-emits.

NOT YET INVESTIGATED. Hypotheses to check: (1) host terminal reflows old content on resize before phux's ED2+repaint, and phux only clears the viewport not scrollback; (2) render_at_full emits content that overlaps an un-cleared region; (3) snapshot/scrollback replay on a resized pane double-writes.

VERIFICATION (NOT tmux — that's circular for a tmux replacement): build a direct PTY harness — allocate a pty (portable_pty), run 'phux attach' in it, write known content, TIOCSWINSZ to resize, read master output, assert no duplicated lines / single coherent frame. Or reason from the repaint code first.
notes: FOLLOW-UP 2026-05-29: the original close (commit 5d80ecb, immediate snapshot on every live resize) fixed discrete resize but introduced a DRAG regime bug — a window drag fires a SIGWINCH storm so the server flooded the client with full snapshots, and a stale-width snapshot corrupts the mirror (duplicated chars while dragging). Fixed in 706fc41 by debouncing the resync (RESIZE_RESYNC_DEBOUNCE=50ms) so a drag coalesces to one snapshot at the settled size. Guard: rapid_resizes_coalesce_into_one_resync_snapshot.
close_reason: Fixed in 831acbb. Root cause: resize reflows server + client mirrors independently; libghostty cols-shrink reflow loses client-mirror content and the live output path never re-sends the historical grid -> lost/duplicated rows. Fix: TerminalActor re-broadcasts a full snapshot (DECSTR+ED2+home reset preamble) on live resize via the output broadcast; ATTACH-time resize suppressed to avoid reordering the handshake. Verified with new raw-PTY harness (crates/phux/examples/pty_resize_probe.rs) + unit regression test resize_rebroadcasts_grid_snapshot_for_phux_8v1. just ci green.

title: [design decided: tmux model] Server lifecycle: pane-close -> remove empty session -> exit server when no sessions remain; add kill-session + phux ls

Without this, an auto-spawned server whose seed pane died before any client attached would self-exit mid-handshake -> user saw "no server". Also silences the spurious "self-exit" log on Ctrl-C shutdown. Tests: server_self_exit.rs now has both served-then-exit and never-served-stays-up cases.

Smoke: `phux kill work` -> next `phux ls` reports "no server running". Companion verbs (kill phux-3kj, ls phux-k61.2) also done.

title: Slim README.md to a router
description:
README.md is BOTH the router and the landing page (the repo page + future docs site index). Two jobs:

1. Draw people in tastefully — phux is pre-alpha but the thesis is real and the prose should land it. Tight hook, a 'why does this matter' beat, status honesty, then the door.
2. Route — once they're in, they need to know exactly which doc answers their next question (concept, spec, contributor, consumer).

Final shape:
- Hook (1 short paragraph + the Hashimoto pull-quote, or replace if there's a better one)
- 'What this is' — 3 bullets max, written like a product page, not a changelog
- 'Why it looks different from tmux' — kept short, links into docs/CONCEPTS.md for depth
- Status — pre-alpha honesty + what works today
- Quickstart — nix develop / just ci / one-line attach
- 'Where to go next' table — small, links into docs/{CONCEPTS, spec, architecture, consumers, vision} + ADR/
- Non-goals (terse) — links to CONTRIBUTING for full list
- License

Target <150 lines. No duplicate prose with CONCEPTS — link instead. Stability: stable; this is the most-read file in the repo and shouldn't churn every wave.
close_reason: README.md slimmed + landing-page polish; QUICKSTART.md created. README is now <130 lines, leads with the thesis + Hashimoto quote, has the smol manifesto, an honest pre-alpha status block, minimal quickstart, and a where-to-go-next router into docs/. Uses HTML-comment metadata so GitHub renders the landing cleanly (CONVENTIONS.md updated to document the exception). QUICKSTART.md gives the run-it-now path with what works / what doesn't.
