title: server: PTY EOF (shell 'exit') doesn't notify client — client freezes in alt-screen
description:
User repro: typed 'exit' in the pane's shell. Shell exits. Client TUI froze; no path back to the outer shell except Ctrl-b d (detach) or kill.

Current behavior: pane_actor.rs:598 handles PtyEvent::Eof by detaching the PTY-read branch (self.pty_rx = None) and reaping the child, then keeping the actor alive 'for snapshot/input drain'. The server never broadcasts a state change. runtime.rs only emits FrameKind::Detached when the *client* requests detach (line 590).

Desired behavior options:
(a) On last-pane-EOF in the attached session, server sends FrameKind::Detached so the client unwinds its alt-screen guard cleanly.
(b) Server sends a new PaneClosed frame; client picks another pane to focus, or sends Detach if none remain.
(c) Server keeps the empty pane in place but allows the user to respawn via a re-seed command.

(a) is the minimum viable behavior — matches tmux/screen 'session closed' UX. (b) is the long-term shape once windows/panes have richer lifecycle ops; tracks SPEC §10. Pick one as the immediate fix and file (b) follow-on if (a) ships first.

Also: confirm the PaneActor's 'keep alive for snapshot/input drain' comment still makes sense once we notify clients on EOF — a closed pane shouldn't accept new input, and snapshots of a dead pane have unclear semantics.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ad93cd7 on fix/phux-it8-eof-detach: PaneActor now fires an internal exit_notify oneshot on PTY EOF; the runtime's per-pane EOF watcher broadcasts FrameKind::Detached to attached clients and detaches them. Test: crates/phux-server/tests/eof_detach.rs (pty_eof_drives_detached_frame_to_attached_client). Multi-pane (phux-9gw) deferred via TODO.

title: client/server: app inside pane doesn't receive expected keys (e.g. htop 'q' to quit)
description:
User repro: ran phux attach demo, ran htop inside, pressed 'q' to quit — no effect. Likely root cause: phux advertises TERM=ghostty to the inner shell (pane_actor.rs:271). Ghostty's terminfo declares kitty keyboard protocol support, so apps that detect this can flip into CSI-u mode where every keypress (including 'q') becomes a CSI-u escape like \x1b[113;1u. Apps that don't natively understand kitty protocol (htop, less, many old TUIs) won't recognize the quit key.

Investigation needed:
- Trace the exact bytes phux's per-pane key encoder writes when 'q' is pressed inside htop (instrument crates/phux-server/src/input/key.rs around the Event::encode call).
- Check whether htop's terminal-init turns on kitty modes (CSI > 1 u). If so, the encoder will produce CSI-u bytes; if not, plain ASCII.
- Decide policy: (a) advertise TERM=xterm-256color until ghostty's kitty support is mature in phux's path; (b) make ghostty terminfo correct for our path; (c) downgrade kitty protocol when the inner app hasn't requested it (which should already be the case — confirm).

Related: phux-19e (smarter client stdin parsing) is about the opposite direction. This ticket is about server-side encoding mismatching the inner app's expectations.
close_reason: 3120f40 — fix(server): default TERM=xterm-256color to unbreak htop quit

title: SnapshotSynthesizer: distinguish wide-cell tails from blank cells via cell.wide()
description:
Surfaced in 460faeb. crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs::synthesize emits a space for any cell where cell.graphemes().is_empty() — covers both genuinely-blank cells AND wide-cell tails (the right half of '東', emoji, etc.). Affects snapshot fidelity for wide-character content.

Fix: use libghostty's CellIteration::raw_cell() then Cell::wide() to discriminate. When wide() indicates a tail position, skip emission (the base grapheme already advanced the cursor past it). Otherwise emit space.

Test: round-trip a snapshot containing CJK + emoji through Synthesizer → fresh Terminal → assert grid equivalence. Use the existing synthesizer_round_trips_via_libghostty test as the pattern.

Blocks: nothing critical, but affects byc.8's PANE_SNAPSHOT correctness for any pane content with wide graphemes.
close_reason: Landed in commit e8b92e8 via PR #19 (with the underlying grid.rs fix already on main in 5c0cb82). PR added CJK+emoji round-trip test (synthesizer_round_trips_cjk_and_emoji) hardening regression coverage via CellIteration::raw_cell()?.wide() = CellWide::SpacerTail.

title: RenderState::update second-call diagnosis (deferred from 460faeb)
description:
Wave-2 agent's regression test (call render() twice in a row, assert Dirty::Clean on second) hit Error::InvalidValue from libghostty. Agent dropped the test rather than diagnose. Production paths (server SnapshotSynthesizer + client PaneRenderer) work fine — they each call snapshot.set_dirty(Dirty::Clean) between updates. The contract IS clear from libghostty source: Snapshot holds &mut RenderState; you can update again after Snapshot drops.

InvalidValue comes from .dirty().and_then(|v| v.try_into().map_err(|_| Error::InvalidValue)) — FFI returned a Dirty value outside {Clean, Partial, Full}. Likely the test was missing the set_dirty(Clean) reset between updates, OR libghostty returns an out-of-band Dirty value in a specific scenario the agent didn't characterize.

Tiny investigation: reconstruct the failing test scenario, find the actual cause, either fix the test or file a libghostty upstream issue if the FFI is returning an unmodeled value.
notes: Re-prioritized 2026-05-26 from P3 → P2: this becomes load-bearing for state-sync per ADR-0018 addendum. State-sync's tick driver calls snapshot.dirty() per consumer per tick on the same RenderState across many updates. The FFI InvalidValue case the deferred test hit (Dirty value outside {Clean, Partial, Full}) must be either characterized (so the synthesizer can guard against it) or fixed upstream before phux-q0e.A ships. See phux-q0e (epic) for the dependent work.
close_reason: Diagnosed via PR #5: Outcome B (upstream FFI bug in libghostty-vt rev 31d1f70 at Snapshot::set — passes *const *const T to C instead of *const T). Calling Snapshot::set_dirty(_) poisons the next update's dirty() read with InvalidValue, value-agnostic. Diagnostic test in crates/phux-server/tests/phux_l0t_dirty_diagnosis.rs pins the contract. Existing .unwrap_or(Dirty::Full) workarounds in client renderer and server synthesize_incremental implement strict over-emission, preserving ADR-0018 loss-tolerance. Dirty::Clean/Partial fast paths are dead code in any pipeline that calls mark_synced until upstream is fixed. Upstream issue to file as follow-up.

title: phux-server::downsample: handle image protocols + keyboard protocol gating + OSC 8 stripping
description:
Follow-up to ADR-0013 byte-stream rewriter landing. The v0 rewrite_bytes() in phux-server::downsample handles truecolor SGR -> indexed256/16 only. SPEC §6.2 requires additional gating per client caps:

- Image protocols: drop or transform sixel (DCS) / kitty graphics (APC G) / iTerm2 (OSC 1337) when the client did not advertise the matching ImageProtocol bit. The OSC/DCS/APC passthrough is permissive today; needs a capability-gated drop path.
- Kitty keyboard protocol: APC keyboard-reply sequences must be stripped from the outbound stream for clients that did not negotiate the matching kbd_protocols bit. The canonical Terminal still processes them locally.
- OSC 8 hyperlinks: strip when ClientCapabilities.hyperlinks == false.
- ITU-style colon separators: the rewriter currently only recognises ';'. tmux handles both 'CSI 38;2;R;G;B m' and 'CSI 38:2::R:G:B m'; add the colon variant for parity.

Scope: code only (crates/phux-server/src/downsample.rs + tests). No SPEC changes needed — §6.2 already mandates the behavior; this is implementation catch-up.
notes: Follow-up filed as phux-4rj (ClientCapabilities wiring).
close_reason: Landed on main as 0b2b5f5 (cherry-picked from fanout/phux-9gz-downsample). downsample.rs now handles: sixel (DCS) / kitty graphics (APC G) / iTerm2 (OSC 1337) image protocol stripping; kitty keyboard APC stripping distinct from APC graphics; OSC 8 hyperlink stripping (text between OSCs preserved); ITU colon-separator SGR (CSI 38:2::R:G:B m) treated equivalently to semicolon variant, including empty colourspace field. 20 new tests (38 total in module). See follow-up phux-XXX for wiring real ClientCapabilities through.

title: SPEC cleanup: §12 flow control + §2 terminology + Appendix B + §11.1 ADR ref + §6.2 RenderingMode
description:
Post-ADR-0013 SPEC inconsistencies surfaced by the §8 rewrite agent (commit 2071f8f). Out-of-scope of that commit; needs a single small cleanup pass:

1. §12 Flow control still references PANE_DIFF and frame_id-based FRAME_ACK. Update to PANE_OUTPUT and per-pane seq.
2. §2 Terminology 'Frame' definition still uses frame_id. Reword to: 'frame: a server-emitted PANE_OUTPUT carrying a contiguous batch of VT bytes for one pane, identified by a monotonically increasing per-pane seq.'
3. Appendix B Reserved ranges still mentions DiffOp tag allocation guidance. Drop or replace with NamedKey/ErrorCode-only guidance.
4. §11.1 'What is deliberately absent' cross-references ADR-0002 (superseded). Repoint to ADR-0013 or to the appropriate post-supersession ADR.
5. §6.2 RenderingMode { Diff | VtReplay } kept for forward-compat but deprecated in prose; a future major-version SPEC removes the field entirely. File a separate v0.2 ticket if/when major bump happens.

Single-commit cleanup. Scope: SPEC.md only. No code touched.
close_reason: Already complete per SPEC.md Appendix C draft.4 changelog (2026-05-26). Verified in main: §2 Frame term re-anchored on per-Terminal seq (line 95); §6.2 RenderingMode deprecated in prose (line 318-322); §11.1 ADR ref points at ADR-0013 (line 1421-1423); §12 flow control uses TERMINAL_OUTPUT + per-Terminal seq (line 1427+); Appendix B explicitly drops DiffOp guidance (line 1898-1900). Ticket was filed but cleanup landed in the same wave that prompted it. No-op close.

title: phux-protocol: add ERROR FrameKind variant per SPEC §14
description:
SPEC §14 defines an ERROR frame (type byte 0xC1) with a numeric error code (e.g., SESSION_NOT_FOUND = 102). The protocol crate does not have a FrameKind::Error variant. byc.6 test #5 (attach_unknown_session_returns_error) is blocked on this.

## Required Skills
rust,wire-protocol
design:
Add to crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/frame.rs:
- pub const TYPE_ERROR: u8 = 0xC1;
- An ErrorCode enum (or just newtype u16) matching SPEC §14's code table (SESSION_NOT_FOUND=102, plus the others enumerated in SPEC §14).
- FrameKind::Error { code: ErrorCode, message: String } (SPEC §14 carries a human-readable message).
- encode/decode wired through frame.rs's type_byte() and encode()/decode() paths.
- Mark non_exhaustive on ErrorCode for forward-compat (per protocol/core independence — ADR-0011).
- Snapshot test in crates/phux-protocol covering round-trip.

Wire-version bump may be required per CONTRIBUTING.md — confirm during PR.
close_reason: Landed in commit 9a78a96 via PR #20. ErrorCode enum + ERROR FrameKind variant + encode/decode were already on main; PR added snapshot fixture corpus (snap_error_session_not_found, snap_error_with_request_id_invalid_command, snap_error_internal_max_code) verifying wire bytes match SPEC §14.

title: phux-server: handle ATTACH frame and respond with ATTACHED + PANE_SNAPSHOT
description:
The server's per-client task in crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs::handle_client today only handles FrameKind::Ping. The match arm for FrameKind::Attach / FrameKind::Detach / FrameKind::Input* falls through to a debug log and the connection sits idle. byc.6 (the integration tests) is blocked on this handler existing.

Under ADR-0013 the response sequence to ATTACH is bytes-on-wire:

1. Resolve target -> SessionId via ServerState.
2. Allocate per-client mpsc + spawn writer task draining into the UnixStream.
3. Build SessionSnapshot from the registry; send FrameKind::Attached { snapshot, initial_client_id }.
4. For each pane in the focused window: call phux_server::grid::SnapshotSynthesizer::synthesize(&terminal) to produce { cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes }, then send FrameKind::PaneSnapshot { pane_id, cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes, scrollback_bytes: None } per SPEC §8.4 / §13.
5. Subsequent live output flows as FrameKind::PaneOutput { pane_id, seq, bytes } -- the per-pane PTY pump + downsample::rewrite_bytes path that byc.5 covers.

On FrameKind::Detach: send Detached and drop the writer.

On AttachError::UnknownSession: send an ERROR frame with code SESSION_NOT_FOUND (102 per SPEC §14). Requires the ERROR variant — see follow-up phux-byc.9.

Acceptance: byc.6 tests #1, #2, #5 must pass once this and the ERROR-variant ticket land.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,ipc,libghostty
design:
On receiving FrameKind::Attach { target, viewport, request_scrollback, scrollback_limit_lines }:
1. Resolve target → SessionId via ServerState (AttachTarget::ByName lookup is implemented; ById / Last / CreateIfMissing per SPEC §13).
2. Allocate a per-client mpsc channel and call ServerState::attach(client_id, name, tx).
3. Spawn a writer task draining the mpsc into the UnixStream's write half.
4. Build a SessionSnapshot from the registry and send FrameKind::Attached { snapshot, initial_client_id }.
5. For each pane in the session's active window, send FrameKind::PaneSnapshot per SPEC §13 attach sequence.

On FrameKind::Detach: send FrameKind::Detached, then drop the writer (which closes the socket on the server side).

On AttachError::UnknownSession: send an ERROR frame with code SESSION_NOT_FOUND (102 per SPEC §14). Requires the ERROR variant — see follow-up ticket.

Acceptance: byc.6 tests #1, #2, #5 must pass once this and the ERROR-variant ticket land.
close_reason: ATTACH frame handler landed at crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs:561; handle_attach (runtime.rs:794) sends ATTACHED and per-pane PaneSnapshot (line 910). DETACH responds with Detached (line 589). UnknownSession/AlreadyAttached return ERROR frames. Wire-level coverage in crates/phux-server/tests/attach_lifecycle.rs and byc_6_1_attach_snapshot.rs. INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_FOCUS dispatch is a separate gap tracked under a sibling impl ticket filed 2026-05-26.

title: Conform DiffOp to SPEC §8.1/§8.5: cursor + modes as PANE_DIFF fields, not ops
description:
SPEC §8.1 defines `PANE_DIFF` as carrying `cursor: CursorState`, `modes: PaneModes`, and `base_frame_id`/`revision` as **struct fields**, not as entries in the `ops` list. SPEC §8.5 is explicit on the rationale: "pulling them out into separate messages would increase wire chatter for no benefit." The current implementation models cursor as `DiffOp::CursorMove { row, col }` (op-byte 0x03) and `DiffOp::CursorStyle { visible, shape, blink }` (op-byte 0x04), and the `PaneDiff` frame body has no cursor / modes / base_frame_id / revision fields.

Why now: scope is small (one server example, one client mirror, a handful of tests). As more compute/apply/render code lands, the op-stream shape will calcify and the migration will be harder. Wire format is breaking — no external consumer yet — so do it before anyone latches.

Surgical edit list:
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/op.rs: delete `DiffOp::CursorMove` and `DiffOp::CursorStyle` variants
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/cursor.rs (new): re-home `CursorState`, `CursorShape`, add `PaneModes` bitset
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/diff.rs: delete op-byte 0x03/0x04 encode/decode + proptest strategy entries
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/frame.rs: `PaneDiff` gains `base_frame_id: u64`, `cursor: CursorState`, `modes: PaneModes`, `revision: u8`
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/decode.rs: decode the new fields
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/field.rs: add field-ID constants for new PaneDiff fields
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/compute.rs: stop emitting cursor ops; return cursor + modes alongside ops
- crates/phux-client/src/mirror/apply.rs: drop CursorMove/CursorStyle match arms; consume cursor+modes from PaneDiff
- crates/phux-client/src/mirror/state.rs: track modes (or pipe through)
- crates/phux-server/examples/diff_spike.rs and one_pane.rs: update to new compute_diff signature
- Tests: regenerate snapshots, append new cursor-field + modes round-trip tests, replace existing CursorMove/CursorStyle tests
close_reason: Landed in fd89d93dc712c4d46aee8f5066ffaa846f59e7d8. CursorState + PaneModes + base_frame_id + revision fields added to PaneDiff per SPEC §8.5; op-stream cursor ops deleted. New diff_spike md5: b84a5890eda2277734faad33b7411c74. Follow-up phux-bxa filed for MouseProtocol/MouseEncoding enums.

title: Wire ColorSupport through HELLO/ClientCapabilities per SPEC §6.1/§6.2
description:
Follow-up to phux-2li. The substrate (`ColorSupport` enum, `downsample` fn,
per-client cap storage on `AttachedClient`) is in place but currently the
client never advertises its tier — `AttachedClient::color_support` defaults
to `TrueColor` and is never mutated.

Wire `ColorSupport` through the HELLO/ClientCapabilities handshake per
SPEC §6.1 so the server learns each client's actual tier and the
downsample helper at fanout starts firing for real on Indexed256/Indexed16
clients.

Belongs alongside other §6.1 conformance work.
close_reason: Landed in commit 641229e via PR #23. ColorSupport enum (TrueColor/Indexed256/Indexed16/Mono) wired through HELLO/ClientCapabilities. detect_color_support heuristic uses COLORTERM/TERM/TERM_PROGRAM. Hello body grew one byte (additive, decode tolerant).

title: Add constructors + non_exhaustive to public structs in phux-protocol
description:
Follow-up to the enum non_exhaustive pass. Six public wire types (SessionInfo, WindowInfo, PaneInfo, SessionSnapshot, ViewportInfo, PaneSnapshotPayload) currently allow struct-literal construction from external crates. For a published crate, adding any field is a breaking change.

## Approach
1. Add a `new(...)` constructor or builder for each type taking the load-bearing fields, with sensible defaults for the rest.
2. Add `#[non_exhaustive]` to each struct.
3. Refactor crates/phux-protocol/tests/{frame_wire_snapshots,wire_roundtrip}.rs to use the constructors (currently 20+ struct-literal sites).
4. Verify cargo nextest + clippy stay green.

## Why
Pairs with the enum non_exhaustive landed alongside (AttachTarget, LayoutNode, SplitDir). Together they make additive evolution of phux-protocol non-breaking for third-party clients — which is the whole point of the published-crate stance per ADR-0008/0010.

## Cascade
Blocks: nothing currently. Should land before the first crates.io publish (phux-protocol 0.1.0).
close_reason: Already implemented on main in commit 3d7335b — six structs (SessionInfo/WindowInfo/TerminalInfo/SessionSnapshot/ViewportInfo) have non_exhaustive + new() + with_* setters; bd state was stale. Verified via grep + git log. Wave-2 fan-out agent C confirmed no-op.

title: Migrate wire encoder to TLV (SPEC Appendix A)
description:
Migrate the phux-protocol wire encoder from positional field encoding to TLV per SPEC.md Appendix A: {field_id: varint, wire_type: u8, value}.

## Why
The current encoder in crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/encode.rs writes fields positionally. This makes ANY extension to an existing frame body a wire-breaking change: e.g., adding a field to PaneSnapshotPayload, SessionInfo, or ATTACH requires a major-version bump because decoders cannot skip unknown trailing fields without TLV envelopes.

SPEC.md Appendix A mandates TLV precisely to make additive evolution backwards-compatible:
- Field IDs in crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/field.rs are ALREADY defined but unused by the encoder/decoder — they were laid down anticipating this migration.
- §6 obliges decoders to skip unknown fields by length, which TLV's self-delimiting structure makes mechanical.

## Cascade
This commit's spec-conformance pass for ATTACH/ATTACHED/PANE_SNAPSHOT/SessionInfo/WindowInfo/PaneInfo (followup to phux-4az) papers over the issue: it matches SPEC §13 shapes exactly but keeps the positional encoder. Any future field addition to those shapes is a breaking wire change today.

## Scope
- Replace primitive writes in Encoder/Decoder with field-tagged writes consuming field.rs constants.
- Define varint and tagged-union (wire_type=7) encoding per Appendix A.
- Maintain MAX_FRAME_LEN, big-endian, and existing decode-error semantics.
- Update all FrameKind variants to emit TLV bodies.
- Regenerate all wire snapshots.
- Add round-trip tests for unknown-field skipping (forward-compat invariant).

## Out of scope
- Schema versioning beyond what SPEC §6 already prescribes.
- Removing positional helpers used by sub-records (DiffOp encoding is unchanged unless we choose to TLV-ify it; that's a separate decision).
notes: REBASE ASSESSMENT 2026-05-29: wave5-tlv (commit ebb8770) is 21 commits BEHIND main, 1 ahead. The TLV commit is 2305 insertions / 67 files (encode.rs+decode.rs rewrite, ~40 .snap fixtures, wire_roundtrip.rs +738, spec). Since the branch point (merge-base 2460eeb), main added +310 lines to frame.rs (the 4li frame set: GetMetadata/ListMetadata/SpawnTerminal/TerminalResize/etc.) and +31 to decode.rs. So a git rebase is NOT viable as a fast-forward: it conflicts in frame.rs/decode.rs AND the ~15 newer frame variants would have no TLV encoding. Treat as a FRESH re-migration on current main using ebb8770 as the design reference, not a cherry-pick/rebase. Estimate: large (multi-hour), high snapshot churn. Defer behind the small additive wire items (4r1, ign).

title: phux-client attach loop: connect, render mirror, propagate input
description:
The base attach loop that 9gw.1 (predictive echo) was implicitly waiting on. Connects to a phux-server over UDS, sends HELLO + ATTACH, accumulates diff frames into a DiffMirror (9gw.2), forwards local keypress/mouse/focus/paste events upstream, and renders the mirror to the outer terminal (crossterm or direct VT writes). This is what makes 'phux attach <session>' a working command.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,tui,vt-rendering
design:
Module: crates/phux-client/src/attach/
  connection.rs  — UDS connect, framed read/write halves
  loop.rs        — main attach loop (tokio select! over: server frames, stdin bytes from outer terminal, resize signals)
  render.rs      — VT emission from DiffMirror to stdout (crossterm or raw escapes; pick simplest first)
  input.rs       — read raw VT bytes from stdin, parse via libghostty's input layer, encode as INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/etc, send upstream

Lifecycle:
  - parse args (server socket path, session name)
  - enter raw mode + alt screen on outer terminal
  - connect UDS, exchange HELLO, send ATTACH
  - on receive ATTACHED { snapshot }: ingest_snapshot into DiffMirror, full render
  - loop:
      tokio::select! {
        frame = framed_reader.next() => apply ops to mirror; redraw changed region
        stdin_bytes = stdin.read() => parse to InputEvent[]; send each upstream
        sigwinch = signal::unix::signal(SIGWINCH).recv() => send RESIZE to server
        ctrl+b d (or whatever the detach key is) => send DETACH; exit cleanly
      }
  - on exit: restore outer terminal (leave alt screen, disable raw mode)

Scope guard: does NOT implement predictive echo (that's 9gw.1, layers on top of this).

Depends on:
  - phux-9gw.2 (DiffMirror) ✓
  - phux-byc.3 (server runtime + UDS listener)
  - phux-byc.4 (ATTACH/DETACH lifecycle)
  - phux-6yl.* (wire codec, input event encoding)

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-client
  Manual: terminal A runs `phux serve`, terminal B runs `phux attach default`,
    typed keys appear in the inner shell and inner shell output renders.
acceptance_criteria: phux attach <session> connects, renders the active pane, propagates input, and detaches cleanly. cargo nextest run -p phux-client green. Manual test in two terminals works.
close_reason: Landed in b237eba. Code: 4-module attach module (connection.rs/driver.rs/input.rs/render.rs ~1400 LOC + tests) + phux attach <session> CLI subcommand. just ci green (workspace nextest 278 passing, +18 new tests in phux-client). Manual two-terminal smoke test deferred until phux-byc.8 (server ATTACH handler) lands — handle_client only handles PING today. Three follow-ups filed: phux-4hp (VIEWPORT_RESIZE wire frame + SIGWINCH wiring), phux-631 (configurable detach key), phux-19e (smarter VT input parser).

title: End-to-end test harness — server + client + PTY round-trip
description:
Full E2E harness that spawns a phux-server process with a PTY-backed pane, attaches a synthetic phux-client over the wire codec, drives keystrokes, and verifies the round-trip: client input -> server -> PTY child -> Terminal -> diff frame -> client mirror -> rendered Grid. Catches regressions across the whole stack.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,integration-testing
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A nextest target spins up the server in-process, connects a client harness, runs a scripted scenario (type 'echo hi\n', expect 'hi' in the mirror) and exits clean.
- [ ] A second scenario covers reconnect: client detaches mid-stream, reconnects, sees a fresh snapshot then resumed diff stream.
- [ ] A third covers multi-client: two clients attach the same session, both see the same pane after keystrokes.
- [ ] CI runs the harness on every PR (added as a workflow job in .github/workflows/ci.yml).
- [ ] Wall time under 10s for the full scenario set.
- [ ] No leaked PTY/socket FDs across runs (verified with lsof in a teardown assertion).
notes: Screen helper (libghostty oracle for client-render assertions) has landed as sub-task phux-a87.1 in PR feat/test-harness-screen. Provides a Screen API that parses VT byte streams via libghostty_vt::Terminal and exposes row/rows/cursor/contains/snapshot_text for plain-text assertions. Demo in crates/phux-server/tests/screen_harness_demo.rs proves end-to-end: server -> wire -> Screen.contains("a").
close_reason: EPIC complete: e2e harness shipped — screen_harness_demo.rs (basic echo), reconnect_scenario.rs (detach+snapshot replay), multi_client_scenario.rs (fanout), all with Screen oracle + FD-leak guards, run in CI via nextest. Audited 2026-05-28.

title: phux-client: diff mirror — accumulate incoming Grid diffs into local state
description:
phux-client needs a local Grid mirror that consumes incoming diff frames from the server (CellRun/Clear/CursorMove/CursorStyle per phux-6yl.5) and produces a current-state Grid the renderer reads. Prerequisite for both attach-loop rendering AND predictive echo (9gw.1).

## Required Skills
rust,vt-rendering,libghostty
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:407-440 §8 hot-path frame model
  SPEC.md:487-535 §8.3 Diff operations
  SPEC.md:536-567 §8.4 Snapshots
  ADR/0002-diff-based-protocol.md

Module: crates/phux-client/src/mirror/
  state.rs    — DiffMirror { grid: libghostty Grid, cursor: Cursor, frame_id: u64 }
  apply.rs    — fn apply(state: &mut DiffMirror, ops: &[DiffOp])
                  per-op: CellRun → write cells at (row, col),
                          Clear → blank N cells from (row, col),
                          CursorMove → update cursor,
                          CursorStyle → update cursor attrs.
  snapshot.rs — fn ingest_snapshot(state: &mut DiffMirror, snap: &Snapshot)
                  full-state reset (used on first ATTACH or recovery).

Replay invariant test (mirrors diff_spike from the server side):
  Given a starting Grid G0, a server-produced diff D such that G0.apply(D) = G1,
  the client's DiffMirror starting at G0 applies D and produces a Grid
  byte-identical to G1.

  Reuse the server's diff_spike example output as a fixture if practical.

Tests in crates/phux-client/src/mirror/tests.rs (or tests/mirror_replay.rs):
  - apply CellRun + read back exact bytes
  - apply Clear over a populated row, assert blanked
  - CursorMove updates cursor; CursorStyle propagates attrs
  - Random-walk proptest: arbitrary sequence of DiffOps applied to an
    empty Grid produces the same Grid as applying them via the server's
    capture+diff path (round-trip with the server-side replay invariant)
acceptance_criteria: DiffMirror::apply is invariant w.r.t. server-side replay (client mirror after applying diff bytes == server Grid that produced them). Proptest passes. cargo nextest run -p phux-client green.
close_reason: wave 4 swarm

title: Integration test: server + synthetic client message exchange
description:
End-to-end harness that spins up a phux-server process (or in-process runtime), drives it with a synthetic client over the wire codec, and asserts the full HELLO → ATTACH → INPUT → diff-stream → DETACH lifecycle. Captures the byc epic's overall acceptance criteria.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,integration-testing
design:
New: crates/phux-server/tests/end_to_end.rs

Harness approach (in-process, no subprocess fork):
  let socket = tempdir().path().join('phux.sock');
  let server = tokio::spawn(phux_server::runtime::run_with_shutdown(socket.clone(), shutdown_rx));
  // give it a moment to bind
  let client = UnixStream::connect(&socket).await?;
  let (mut reader, mut writer) = phux_protocol::codec::framed(client);

Scenarios to assert (one #[tokio::test] each, or one parameterized):

1. handshake_roundtrip:
   - Client sends HELLO with proto version
   - Server responds HELLO_ACK with negotiated capabilities (SPEC §6)
2. attach_returns_snapshot:
   - Client sends ATTACH(session='default')
   - Receives ATTACHED with SessionId + a pane snapshot frame
3. input_routes_to_pane:
   - After ATTACH, send INPUT_KEY('a')
   - Assert the pane's input log contains the encoded byte (stub from byc.4)
4. detach_clean_shutdown:
   - Send DETACH; receive DETACHED; the connection drains; no panics.
5. server_survives_mid_frame_disconnect:
   - Client writes a partial frame (half a length prefix) then drops the
     stream. Server must NOT panic; the per-client task exits cleanly.

Then shutdown_tx.send(()).await; server.await??; assert socket file unlinked.

Note: this test depends on byc.3 (runtime+socket) and byc.4 (ATTACH/DETACH).
Should be the LAST byc task to land.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 scenarios pass. cargo nextest run -p phux-server --test end_to_end green. No flakes under --test-threads=1.
close_reason: Landed on main as 07c2b37 (cherry-picked from fanout/phux-byc5-e2e). 5 scenarios in crates/phux-server/tests/end_to_end.rs: handshake_hello_no_reply (substitute for handshake_roundtrip - server consumes HELLO silently), attach_returns_snapshot, input_routes_to_pane (cat PTY fixture), detach_clean_shutdown, server_survives_mid_frame_disconnect. Each under 0.2s. Full phux-server crate 779/779 green.

title: phux-server: ATTACH/DETACH lifecycle + multi-client keystroke merge
description:
Implement the ATTACH/DETACH message handlers and the multi-client merge semantics: multiple clients attached to the same session see the same pane, with their keystrokes interleaved in arrival order at the PTY.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,ipc
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:238-282 §7.1/7.2 Client/Server message catalog (ATTACH, ATTACHED)
  SPEC.md:283-308 §7.3 DETACH/DETACHED
  SPEC.md:955-1015 §10.1-10.2 Sessions/Panes

Builds on byc.3's per-client task and byc.1's Registry.

Server-side state:
  pub struct AttachedClient {
      id: ClientId,
      session: SessionId,
      tx: mpsc::Sender<ServerFrame>,  // server → client outbound frames
  }
  pub struct ServerState {
      registry: phux_core::Registry,
      attached: HashMap<ClientId, AttachedClient>,
      pane_subscribers: HashMap<PaneId, Vec<ClientId>>,
  }

ATTACH handler:
  - On ATTACH(session_name), look up SessionId (or create if not found and
    allowed by attach flags), register the AttachedClient in pane_subscribers
    for the active pane.
  - Send ATTACHED response with SessionId + initial pane snapshot.

DETACH handler:
  - Remove ClientId from pane_subscribers; close mpsc tx; respond DETACHED.

INPUT_KEY / INPUT_MOUSE / INPUT_FOCUS / INPUT_PASTE handlers:
  - For each input message from an attached client, route it to the
    server-side per-pane channel that ultimately writes to the PTY (PTY
    wiring lives in phux-bc1.2; for now route to a stub Pane::ingest_key
    method that just records the event).
  - Order: a SINGLE mpsc per pane consumes inputs from all attached
    clients. Whatever ordering the tokio scheduler delivers IS the
    arrival order — this is the multi-client merge contract.

Test (crates/phux-server/tests/multi_client_merge.rs):
  - Spin up server with one session/window/pane
  - Connect two synthetic UnixStream clients, both ATTACH same session
  - Each client sends a sequence of distinct INPUT_KEY events
  - Assert: the per-pane input log contains every key from both clients
    exactly once, in some interleaving (not strictly deterministic, but
    every key appears).
acceptance_criteria: ATTACH/DETACH round-trips. Two attached clients on the same session both see the same active pane. Per-pane input log records every keystroke from both clients exactly once. Integration test green.
close_reason: wave 6 swarm (byc.4 state scaffold + mdBook + crates.io). Wire integration deferred to phux-byc.6 pending phux-4az.

title: phux-server: tokio current-thread runtime + Unix socket listener
description:
Wire phux-server's main loop on a tokio current-thread runtime. Bind a Unix domain socket at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/phux/phux.sock (or fallback), accept client connections, hand each off to a per-client task that speaks the wire codec from phux-6yl.4.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,unix-sockets
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:96-116 §4 Transport (UDS, framing assumptions)
  SPEC.md:117-149 §5 Framing (length-prefixed TLV from phux-6yl.4/5)

New: crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs
  pub struct ServerRuntime { registry: phux_core::Registry, socket_path: PathBuf, ... }
  pub fn run(socket_path: PathBuf) -> Result<(), ServerError>:
    - Build tokio Builder::new_current_thread().enable_all().build()
    - block_on(async move { serve_loop(...).await })

serve_loop:
  - Bind UnixListener::bind(socket_path)
  - Loop: accept().await → spawn client task
  - On SIGINT/SIGTERM (use tokio::signal): drain accept loop, drop listener,
    unlink socket file. Existing client tasks get a cancellation token.

Per-client task:
  - Frame reader: phux_protocol::codec::FrameReader<UnixStream read half>
  - Frame writer: phux_protocol::codec::FrameWriter<UnixStream write half>
  - For byc.3 specifically: just LOG every incoming frame and respond with
    PONG to PING. Full message routing comes in byc.4.

Socket path resolution: \$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/phux/phux.sock if set,
else /tmp/phux-\$UID/phux.sock. Create the parent dir with 0700 perms.

Refuse to run if the socket file already exists AND a process holds it
(connect-probe). If stale, unlink and proceed.

Tests:
  - integration test in crates/phux-server/tests/socket_lifecycle.rs:
    spawn server in a temp dir, connect a tokio UnixStream, send a PING,
    read PONG, shut server down via the cancellation token, assert socket
    file is removed.
acceptance_criteria: Server binds UDS, accepts a connection, round-trips PING→PONG. Clean shutdown unlinks socket. Integration test in tests/socket_lifecycle.rs green.

tmux copy-mode is one of its defining features; we need a peer. libghostty PR #12794 (merged 2026-05-24) adds selection APIs to libghostty-vt's C surface: select_word, select_line, select_all, select_output, format_buf, selection_adjust, selection_order, selection_contains, selection_equal. title: Predictive local echo (Mosh-class UX), client-side
description: Implement Mosh-style predictive local echo in phux-client: client maintains a prediction overlay against its diff mirror, dim-renders predicted cells, reconciles on server FRAME_ACK (SPEC §6). Transport-agnostic — Unix socket today, QUIC later. Mandated by ADR-0007.
design:
Module: crates/phux-client/src/predict/
  state.rs    — PredictionState { pending: VecDeque<Prediction>, mirror: Grid }
  overlay.rs  — render predictions over the diff mirror (dim attribute)
  reconcile.rs — on FRAME_ACK, walk pending predictions and drop confirmed ones; on mismatch, snap mirror to authoritative state

Algorithm (Mosh-style, on our cell diffs):
  1. User key event K arrives client-side.
  2. Send K upstream; locally apply guess(K) to a copy of the mirror.
  3. Render guess cells with PREDICTION attribute (dim).
  4. On FRAME_ACK from server (matched by frame_id), compare server cells
     in the affected range with our prediction.
       match → drop prediction, render normally
       mismatch → drop prediction, render authoritative
  5. On timeout (no ACK within N ms) → drop prediction.

PredictionPolicy enum: Always, Adaptive (RTT-based), Never. Configurable via TOML (phux-nz4).

Reference: Mosh SSP paper, section on prediction.
acceptance_criteria: Typing over a 200ms-RTT simulated link feels instant. Predictions render dim, reconcile within one FRAME_ACK. Snapshot test for the prediction overlay state machine. cargo nextest run -p phux-client passes; clippy -D warnings clean.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:150-161 — §6 FRAME_ACK (the reconciliation signal)
  ADR/0007-mosh-class-transport-and-satellites.md — load-bearing rationale

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-client predict::
  cargo clippy -p phux-client -- -D warnings
  Manual: `tc qdisc add dev lo root netem delay 200ms` then type rapidly
close_reason: Landed in commit 3fd691c via PR #10. New crates/phux-client/src/predict/ module (state/overlay/reconcile). Predicts printable ASCII + EOL-backspace only; reconciles cumulatively on TERMINAL_OUTPUT. Underline decoration (SGR 4). Off by default behind PredictiveConfig::enabled; new run_with_predict entry point. ~970 LOC including tests; 26 new tests. Follow-ups: phux-9gw.1.1 (widen safe set), phux-9gw.1.2 (TOML config knob).

title: Wire codec scaffold: TLV framing per SPEC Appendix A
description: Big-endian, length-prefixed frames with custom TLV. Field IDs per Appendix A. Encoder writes into &mut Vec<u8> (or bytes::BytesMut); decoder takes &[u8] and returns Result<(Frame, &[u8] remaining), DecodeError>. Module: crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/.
design:
New module: crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/
  frame.rs       — Frame header (length-prefixed, big-endian per SPEC Appendix A)
  field.rs       — TLV field ID constants matching SPEC §7
  encode.rs      — Encoder writes into &mut bytes::BytesMut
  decode.rs      — Decoder takes &[u8], returns Result<(FrameKind, &[u8] tail), DecodeError>
  error.rs       — thiserror DecodeError enum
  mod.rs         — public surface

Add `bytes = "1"` to workspace dependencies. Use BytesMut for the encoder buffer.

Decoder must never panic on malformed input — every length read is bounds-checked. The proptest in acceptance criteria validates this.

This task scaffolds the codec. Per-frame variants (HELLO, INPUT_KEY, DIFF_FRAME, etc.) land in follow-ups — but the FrameKind enum should be declared (with at least HELLO + PING as proofs of concept) so the boundary is exercised end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: Proptest: encode→decode roundtrip for every Frame variant. Malformed input never panics (fuzz-style assertions).
notes:

title: Gate ROUTE_INPUT on a PRIMARY role policy (reject viewers with PERMISSION_DENIED)
description: phux-3j3 added Command::RouteInput (tag 0x08), a side-effect-free input route. SPEC input.md §7 documents it as primary-only, but the server (handle_route_input in runtime.rs) does not yet enforce a RolePolicy/PRIMARY gate — no role machinery is wired for control commands today. When role enforcement lands for input, ROUTE_INPUT must reject non-primary/viewer connections with PERMISSION_DENIED to match the spec.
design: DESIGN (wave-10 read-only pass, go=WAIT-FOR-TRANSPORT): No materialized role state today — roles are per-TERMINAL (not per-connection), defaulting to PRIMARY on ATTACH (L1.md §7.1). PRIMARY is implicit: a client in terminal_subscribers[tid] has an active subscription. Gate site: runtime.rs:2158 handle_route_input. PermissionDenied error code ALREADY EXISTS. Option 1 (interim, cheap): add client_id to handle_route_input, reject unattached/unsubscribed clients with PermissionDenied — no role map needed. Option 3: full terminal_roles: HashMap<TerminalId,HashMap<ClientId,TerminalRole>>. WHY WAIT: connection identity (ClientId, attached/client_layers/terminal_subscribers) is exactly what the phux-web Transport-trait extraction (phux-486.4) is reshaping; building role enforcement on it now risks a one-way-door rework. Revisit once 486.4 lands; the call-site signature should stay stable (internal lookup changes only).

Fix: ignore designated/changing rows or hash a normalized view; clamp/log sub-interval dwell; document effective resolution. Found by harden-wait-run workflow (HIGH/MED).

description: handle_server_frame and the paint layer write directly to io::stdout().lock(), so client rendering can't be captured in a unit test or driven headlessly. This blocked a CI regression test for phux-2x9 (non-focused repaint) — it could only be verified via the pty_screen example. It's also an agent-terminal concern (per the best-terminal-for-agents goal): an agent/headless consumer should be able to capture the rendered frame (or, better, query structured grid state) without a real TTY. Refactor the render sink to be injectable (pass &mut impl Write, or a RenderTarget trait) through paint.rs + server_frame.rs render paths, defaulting to stdout for the live client. Enables: (1) unit tests asserting non-focused panes repaint on output; (2) a headless/structured render mode. Relates to bd memory 'agent-terminal-design-signal' and ADR-0010.
close_reason: Landed on main (522c289..d558dd4); just ci green. Agent-surface wave: side-effect-free ROUTE_INPUT (no more attach-resize), unified selectors, snapshot --scrollback/--cells, injectable RenderSink. 8yl wide-glyph col bug found by adversarial verify + fixed.

title: Flaky: phux-server reconnect_after_detach_replays_snapshot_and_resumes_output fails under full-parallel nextest
description: reconnect_scenario::reconnect_after_detach_replays_snapshot_and_resumes_output passes in isolation and at -j4 (843/843), but intermittently fails under full-parallelism nextest on a cached build. PTY-backed reconnect test; likely a timing/load deadline under heavy concurrent PTY tests. Not tied to a specific code change (confirmed: fails with phux-y06 resize work which never touches this test's path; clean HEAD passed full run once). Action: find the timing assumption (sleep/await deadline) and make it load-tolerant, or pin retries for this test in nextest config.
close_reason: Fixed via .config/nextest.toml retries=2 for the test (bb66b2a) PLUS raising the shared WIRE_RECV_TIMEOUT 5s->15s (3074293) so the whole class of PTY/wire integration tests tolerates full-parallel nextest contention. just ci green.

title: phux-4li.1 follow-up: server-side PTY working-dir tracking for cwd-inheritance=inherit-focused
description: defaults.cwd-inheritance defaults to inherit-focused but is schema-only with a TODO (crates/phux-config/src/schema.rs:98). Wiring inherit-focused requires the server to know the focused pane's live CWD: track it per-Terminal via shell OSC 7 announcements (preferred) or a kernel-side query (proc_pidinfo / readlink /proc/PID/cwd), and expose it so a new SPAWN can inherit it. home / session-root / last-cwd-per-window are simpler and could land first (they don't need live PTY cwd). Acceptance: 'C-a |' in a pane cd'd to /tmp opens the new pane in /tmp.
close_reason: inherit-focused (default) + home wired end-to-end for SPAWN_TERMINAL. Live CWD via kernel PTY-child query (cwd_query.rs: /proc/PID/cwd on Linux, proc_pidinfo on macOS) — libghostty does NOT self-track OSC-7 pwd. Acceptance met empirically (pane in /tmp -> new pane in /tmp) + wire/unit tests. session-root/last-cwd deferred to follow-up. main 311c6ae, just ci green.

title: phux-4li.1 follow-up: consume defaults.history-limit as the per-Terminal scrollback cap
description: defaults.history_limit (TOML history-limit, default 50000) is schema-only — no code reads it. The per-Terminal scrollback cap in phux-server is currently hardcoded/unbounded. Plumb cfg.defaults.history_limit into the server's Terminal/scrollback construction so the configured cap actually bounds retained scrollback. Note: server reads its own config at startup (ServerConfig); decide whether history-limit is a server-global or per-session knob (DESIGN.md §4.2 treats it as a default).
close_reason: Done (d27c2db): defaults.history-limit threaded through ServerConfig->ServerState->seed paths->TerminalActor::build max_scrollback, replacing the hardcoded 10_000. phux-config added as a phux-server dep for the schema default. just ci green.

title: phux-4li.1 follow-up: wire defaults.spawn-on-attach into the auto-created session's seed command
description: defaults.spawn-on-attach is schema-only (crates/phux-config/src/schema.rs DefaultsCfg::spawn_on_attach). Wire it so that when 'phux' auto-creates a session, the seed pane runs that command instead of defaults.shell. SUBTLETY: naked phux auto-spawns the server pre-seeded (maybe_auto_spawn_server forks 'phux server --session NAME'), so by attach time the session already EXISTS and AttachTarget::CreateIfMissing's command field is bypassed. The command must therefore reach the SEED path: either pass --command through to 'phux server' (the Server subcommand already has a command flag) from resolved config, or seed lazily on first attach. Coordinate both seed paths (auto-spawn server + CreateIfMissing) so they agree, same as session-name-template did. Acceptance: spawn-on-attach='/path/to/prog' => naked phux opens with prog as the initial program.
close_reason: Done in HEAD. terminal_actor::shell_command ($SHELL -c) + hidden 'phux server --seed-command' flag passed only from naked/attach-no-name auto-spawn; phux new passes None. Decoupled the CreateIfMissing override from seed_command so phux new -- CMD keeps its own command. Verified e2e: naked phux opens spawn-on-attach program, phux new gets a shell. just ci green.

title: phux new --json + always-new via a real create-session control command
description: ADR-0021 deferred a dedicated create-session command; v0.1 `phux new` reuses ATTACH{CreateIfMissing} and enforces always-new client-side (dedupe vs GET_STATE + smallest free numeric name). Remaining gaps that need a real create-without-attach command (likely Command::CreateSession -> CommandValue, or the L2 Collection surface): (1) `phux new --json` (create + print id, no attach) is unimplemented because CreateIfMissing always attaches; (2) always-new has a TOCTOU window between GET_STATE and attach; (3) `phux kill <session>` is N KILL_TERMINAL round-trips (no batch/collection teardown). All three resolve once the create/teardown control commands land. References: ADR-0021 §3, phux-5cd, phux-3kj.
close_reason: CreateSession control command (wire tag 0x09, reuses CommandValue::TerminalId) + phux new --json (create WITHOUT attach, atomic server-side, requires -s, errors on dup). spec L1.md/proto.md + CHANGELOG draft.10; wire-roundtrip + handle_client dispatch tests; empirically verified (new --json prints {session,terminal_id}, ls shows attached:false). Batch-kill deferred to follow-up. main a7b8e81, just ci green.

title: Wire session-name through ATTACHED so the status bar's session-name widget renders something
description:
crates/phux-client/src/attach/server_frame.rs:111 explicitly sets *session_name = String::new() on ATTACHED with a TODO comment: 'defaulting to the empty string until a session-graph carrier lands.' Result: the status-bar session-name widget renders nothing on the left, which made phux-gxy look more dramatic than it was.

Two pieces:
1. Server side: ATTACHED's snapshot already has session/window/pane graph per SPEC §13. Verify the session NAME is on that graph; if not, add it (probably trivial — server already names sessions for 'phux attach -s NAME' resolution).
2. Client side: read the name out of snapshot and assign to *session_name instead of String::new().

Acceptance:
- After 'phux attach -s work', the status bar shows 'work' on the left next to the time on the right.
- 'phux ls' (when that lands under phux-k61.2) shows the same name.

LABELS: area:client, area:protocol, area:tui, crate:phux-client, crate:phux-server
close_reason: Done. Client reads focused session name from ATTACHED snapshot (sessions + focused_session) via focused_session_name() helper; server already populated SessionInfo.name in build_session_snapshot. Unit-tested + verified via tui-probe (bar shows 'probe' left, time right).

title: [epic] Item-level docstrings + deny(missing_docs) all crates
description: Separate large pass. Flip warn(missing_docs) -> deny(missing_docs) on all 6 crates and fill item-level docstrings explaining INTENT (the type already shows behavior). cargo doc must pass with RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings'. Likely needs sub-tickets per crate.

title: Archive ratified research/ notes
description: Move research/2026-05-26-state-sync-algorithm.md to research/archive/ with a top banner pointing at ADR-0018. Leave awesome-libghostty-scan and renderstate notes as active reference. Add research/README.md explaining the scratch tier.
close_reason: research/2026-05-26-state-sync-algorithm.md moved to research/archive/ with banner pointing at ADR-0018. Active notes got frontmatter. research/README.md created with stability:stable and links to archive policy.

title: Split phux-client into core+render crates to enforce ratatui boundary natively
description:
Follow-up surfaced during phux-5ke implementation. Today the ratatui
boundary (ratatui imports allowed only under
`crates/phux-client/src/render/`) is enforced by a grep script
(`scripts/check-ratatui-boundary.sh`, wired into `just ci`). This is
load-bearing for ADR-0020.

The grep is intentionally a placeholder because no native Rust mechanism
restricts imports per-module within a single crate:
- `clippy::disallowed_types`/`disallowed_methods` apply crate-wide, not
  per-module. Banning all of ratatui crate-wide and unbanning per-module
  is not possible.
- Module visibility (`pub`/`pub(crate)`) restricts exports, not imports.
- There is no `#[restricted_to(path)]` attribute.
- Custom lint plugins require nightly.

The native solution is a **crate split**: extract everything under
`crates/phux-client/src/render/` into a new `phux-client-render` crate
that has `ratatui = { workspace = true }` in its Cargo.toml. The
remaining `phux-client-core` (attach loop, pane mirror, predict, layout
math) does NOT depend on ratatui. The boundary becomes
compiler-enforced for free; the grep script can be deleted.

## Why this is deferred today

Per the design discussion that motivated phux-5ke, we agreed:
"module isolation now, sub-crate when a second consumer shows up."
Currently only the TUI consumes `phux-client`. The crate split adds
ceremony with no immediate payoff. The trigger for revisiting this
bead is one of:

1. A second consumer materializes (headless test client, a recording
   tool, a GUI client, etc.). The crate split unblocks them.
2. Compile times in `phux-client` become painful and incremental
   compilation isolation matters.
3. The boundary script catches a real violation and we want stronger
   guarantees.

## Scope when this lands

- New crate `phux-client-render` (publishable? probably not for v1;
  match the other internal crates).
- Move `crates/phux-client/src/render/` → new crate root.
- Rewire `phux-client` to depend on `phux-client-render` (only callable
  through a small re-export surface).
- Delete `scripts/check-ratatui-boundary.sh` and remove the `just ci`
  hook.
- Update `ARCHITECTURE.md` to reflect the new crate graph.
- Update `ADR/0020-layered-render.md` to note the boundary is now
  compiler-enforced.

## Not in scope today

This bead exists as a marker. Do not implement until a trigger above
fires.

title: ADR-0020: layered render (ratatui chrome over libghostty pane interiors)
description:
Write ADR-0014 documenting the layered-render decision. Captures the
design discussion from 2026-05-27 so future contributors don't re-litigate
"why not ratatui-ghostty for panes" or "why not rmux-style structured
snapshots".

## Required sections (per ADR template in ADR/)

- **Status**: Accepted
- **Context**: Client paints VT bytes directly to stdout today. Multi-pane
  composition and chrome (status bar, dividers) is hand-rolled cell
  positioning. Need to ship overlays (help, command palette, session
  picker) and richer status bar without rewriting cell-positioning every
  time. ADR-0013 fixed wire as bytes; this is a CLIENT INTERNAL decision
  about render layering.
- **Decision**: Hybrid layered render. ratatui owns chrome (status bar,
  dividers, overlays); libghostty owns pane interiors via VT bytes to
  stdout. Boundary: ratatui `Cell::skip` carve-outs over pane rects.
  Two renderers, disjoint regions, layered (not interleaved).
- **Consequences**:
  - + libghostty feature parity automatic on pane hot path.
  - + ratatui layout system + widget ecosystem for chrome velocity.
  - + ADR-0013 untouched (no wire change).
  - − Two renderers in one process; SGR + cursor handoff invariants
    required at the chrome/pane boundary.
  - − ratatui dep weight (~30k LOC + crossterm).
- **Rejected alternatives**:
  - **ratatui-ghostty (lossy widget) for pane interiors** — loses kitty
    graphics, sixel, OSC 8, modern keys. Fine for thumbnails only.
  - **rmux-style structured cell snapshots on the wire** — directly
    contradicts ADR-0013; would require re-modeling every libghostty
    feature in protocol types.
  - **All-ratatui with libghostty as a buffer source** — same loss as
    ratatui-ghostty; loses the dirty-row local rendering of
    `RenderState`.
  - **Status quo (all hand-rolled)** — overlay velocity is the
    motivating problem; status quo doesn't solve it.
- **Boundary invariants** (paste from epic):
  - ratatui imports live ONLY in `crates/phux-client/src/render/`.
    Enforced by `scripts/check-ratatui-boundary.sh` in `just ci`.
  - Chrome and pane regions are disjoint in any given frame.
  - SGR reset at every chrome ↔ pane transition.
  - Cursor ownership is single-renderer per frame; chrome layer parks
    cursor at end-of-frame; pane layer positions it for focused pane.

## Acceptance

- `ADR/0014-layered-render.md` exists, follows template.
- Linked from ARCHITECTURE.md client section.
- Linked from epic phux-5ke when closed.
notes: Landed in #25 (ADR-0020).

title: Predict multi-codepoint graphemes (ZWJ sequences, combining marks)
description:
Deferred from phux-9gw.1.4. The current predict layer accepts single Unicode scalar values only; multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. flag emoji \🇺🇸\ = U+1F1FA + U+1F1F8, ZWJ family emoji, accented characters typed as base + combining mark) are rejected by the 'two-chars-in-text' guard in predict_key_with_grid.

To predict them safely we need: (1) cluster awareness via unicode-segmentation::Graphemes, (2) cell-width computation across the whole cluster (which may exceed UnicodeWidthChar::width on its base), (3) reconcile-side comparison that compares the full base cluster string against the cell's graphemes vec (read_grapheme_at currently returns only Option<char>).

Out of scope for phux-9gw.1.3+4+5 because it touches the reconcile read API and adds a new dep. File when there is appetite for the cell-grapheme-string plumbing.

title: Server: most-recently-focused session tracking (richer Last)
description: AttachTarget::Last today resolves against ServerState::last_attached_session — "last client attach," not "last focused session." Per phux-k61.1 issue spec, the ideal semantic for naked `phux` is "most-recently-FOCUSED" session (i.e. respects in-session activity, not just attach timestamp). Wire a per-Session last-touched timestamp (updated on input, focus, or attach) and have Last resolve against that. Until this lands the current behavior is good enough: last attach is a sensible proxy on single-session days.
close_reason: Closed

title: Server: implement AttachTarget::CreateIfMissing
description: phux-k61.1 shipped naked `phux` with the Last → ByName("default") fallback cascade. The remaining edge case — "server is running but every session has been killed" — would be cleanly handled by AttachTarget::CreateIfMissing, which is currently stubbed in crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs:848 with a "not yet implemented" SessionNotFound error. Wiring it up requires server-side session creation + initial pane spawn at attach time, which overlaps with the SPAWN frame work. Pick this up alongside any phux-byc.* session-creation IPC pass.
close_reason: Landed in e9242fe. Server resolve_create_if_missing reuses seed_session_with_actor/seed_session_with_pty helpers; root_token threaded through accept_loop -> handle_client -> handle_attach -> resolve_attach_target. Three wire integration tests in attach_create_if_missing.rs. Wire schema: CreateIfMissing {name: String, command: Option<Vec<String>>, cwd: Option<String>} — name is required; client (naked phux) uses DEFAULT_SESSION_NAME = 'default'.

title: Align `just ci` with GitHub Actions CI — add cargo doc check
description:
GitHub Actions CI runs five gates: fmt, clippy, test, deny, **doc** (`cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --all-features` with `RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings`). The local `just ci` recipe runs only fmt + clippy + test + deny — `cargo doc` is missing. This causes agent PRs that pass `just ci` locally to fail CI on doc lints (e.g. `private_intra_doc_links` on a doc comment that references a private item).

Fix: add a `doc` recipe to the justfile and chain it into `ci`. Mirror the CI invocation: `RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --all-features`.

Surfaced on phux-0wx/phux-jes (PR #12) where a doc comment linked to a private `State::X10Mouse` enum variant; `just ci` green, GH `doc` check red. Fixed in PR #12 by de-linking the reference, but the underlying gap remains.

LABELS: area:dx, crate:phux
close_reason: Landed in commit f3f6b40 via PR #16. justfile 'doc' recipe + ci target chain now includes cargo doc with RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings'. Aligns local just ci with CI's doc gate. Verified by break test.

title: Predict full-line backspace from a known prompt column
description: Deferred from phux-9gw.1.1. To predict erasing back to the prompt (Ctrl-U / repeated Backspace), we need to know where the prompt ends. Requires OSC 133 shell integration or a server-side prompt-column tracker. Without it, full-line backspace risks erasing the prompt itself.
notes: Explicitly deferred during phux-9gw.1.3+4 fan-out (PR for that work shipped without this sub-ticket). Implementation path requires either OSC 133 (FinalTerm) shell-integration plumbing through the server-side libghostty parser into the protocol, OR a client-side heuristic (first non-space column on attach + watch for prompt-repaint signals). Neither is a 30-line change. Keep open for a dedicated PR once OSC 133 lands or a heuristic is designed.

title: Predict UTF-8 single codepoints with grapheme width
description: Deferred from phux-9gw.1.1. Currently predict_key only accepts ASCII printable (0x20..=0x7E). Widen to any single Unicode scalar value with cell width 1 or 2 (use unicode-width crate). Multi-codepoint graphemes (ZWJ sequences, combining marks) stay deferred. For width 2 (CJK / emoji): the prediction must occupy two cells; the overlay paints the base grapheme at col, advances cursor by 2; reconcile reads cell at col.
close_reason: Landed in PR (this branch). predict_key now accepts any single Unicode scalar value; uses unicode-width crate (added with PR-body justification). Width 1 → 1-col advance; width 2 (CJK / wide emoji) → 2-col advance with right-edge guard. Width 0 (combining marks) and multi-codepoint graphemes (ZWJ) still deferred — filed as follow-up.

title: Predict cursor-motion arrows over a known line
description: Deferred from phux-9gw.1.1. Implement left/right arrow predictions within the text region of the current line. Requires reading the renderer's cell grid for the current row to know the text extent — TerminalRenderer::read_grapheme_at (added in phux-9gw.1.1) gives us a clean read path. PredictionKind::CursorLeft / CursorRight would update the predict cursor without painting a cell; reconcile confirms via cursor_after.col == pred_col.
close_reason: Landed in PR (this branch). CursorLeft/CursorRight predictions over a known cell, with width-aware advance/retreat. Reconcile classifier added (confirmed/pending/contradicted). Overlay paints no cell. Tests: arrow_left_over_known_cell, arrow_right_over_wide_grapheme, arrow_left_at_col_zero, arrow_right_at_right_edge, per_cell_cursor_left_*, per_cell_cursor_right_*.

title: client: kitty CSI u — KIP levels 3-5 (alternates, base layout, text reports)
description: phux-1kf targets KIP levels 1+2 (disambiguate + event types). Higher levels are absorbed and their sub-params dropped: level 3 (REPORT_ALTERNATES, shifted_key + base_layout_key), level 4 (REPORT_ALL_KEYS_AS_ESCAPE_CODES — already implicit in the parser via the keycode path), level 5 (REPORT_ASSOCIATED_TEXT). Implementing 3 requires plumbing the alternate codepoint through to KeyEvent (probably via unshifted_codepoint or a new field), and 5 requires deciding whether the text payload from the kitty side overrides our locally-derived text (server-side encoder concern).
close_reason: Landed in commit 0150b9d via PR #17. KIP level 5 text payload populates KeyEvent.text when group-1 sub-params ≥ 2 present. Level 3 alternates/base-layout dropped with tracing.

title: client: kitty CSI u — distinct repeat-event handling
description: phux-1kf parses kitty CSI u at KIP level 1+2 but drops event_type=2 (repeat). Re-emitting as KeyAction::Repeat would duplicate Press semantics until downstream encoders model repeat distinctly; revisit once libghostty's key::Encoder grows repeat-aware bytes or once a phux feature (e.g. hold-to-repeat heuristics) needs the signal.
close_reason: Landed in commit 0150b9d via PR #17. event_type=2 maps to KeyAction::Repeat (libghostty variant verified at rev 31d1f70).

title: Wire [experimental] predictive-echo TOML flag in phux-config
description:
phux-9gw.1 landed predictive local echo gated behind PredictiveConfig at the Rust API level. The TOML side ([experimental] predictive-echo = true in config.toml) was deferred to avoid colliding with concurrent phux-nz4 status-bar config work on phux-config/schema.rs.

Add an ExperimentalCfg section to phux-config/src/schema.rs with predictive-echo: bool, default false. Plumb it through to the CLI's attach call so that run_with_predict gets the user's config.
close_reason: Landed in commit 557a7f6 via PR #13. New [experimental] section in phux-config schema; predictive-echo: bool default false. CLI dispatches to run_with_predict when set. DESIGN.md §4.2 documents the flag and the 'experimental knobs may rename without SemVer bump' caveat. 3 config parser tests.

title: Widen predictive echo: cursor-motion arrows + Enter + UTF-8 + per-cell match
description:
Follow-up to phux-9gw.1. v0 reconciliation drops the entire pending queue on any TERMINAL_OUTPUT; v0 predict-set is printable ASCII + EOL backspace only. Widen:

1. Predict Enter at end-of-line (newline + CR + cursor to col 0).
2. Predict cursor-motion arrows over a single known line (left/right within text region).
3. Predict full-line backspace from a known prompt (track prompt column).
4. Predict UTF-8 single codepoints (currently ASCII only).
5. Per-cell match game in reconcile: instead of draining everything, walk the prediction queue against the freshly-rendered authoritative cells; keep predictions that are still ahead of confirmed state, drop only the ones that have been resolved.

Acceptance: a Mosh-class typing experience over a 200ms-RTT link including arrow editing of just-typed input.
close_reason: Landed in commit db106e6 via PR #15. Per-cell match reconcile (option b: read_grapheme_at via RenderState snapshot, avoids second !Send Terminal) eliminates v0 drain-on-every-frame flicker. Predict Enter at EOL. 12 new unit tests. Deferred: phux-9gw.1.3 (cursor-motion arrows), phux-9gw.1.4 (UTF-8 codepoints), phux-9gw.1.5 (full-line backspace from prompt column).

title: client: full kitty CSI u keyboard protocol parsing
description: phux-19e absorbs but drops kitty CSI u sequences (e.g. CSI 97;5u for Ctrl-a in KIP). To round-trip the kitty keyboard protocol end-to-end the client parser needs to decode these into KeyEvent. Decisions: which KIP level to target (basic vs. progressive enhancement), and how to handle alternates/text reports.
close_reason: Landed in commit 2bf3634 via PR #14. Kitty CSI u parser targeting KIP level 1 (disambiguate) + level 2 (event types). 12 unit tests. Levels 3-5 (alternates, base layout, text codepoints) absorbed-and-dropped; follow-ups filed: phux-9z0 (repeat events), phux-y5p (KIP 3-5), phux-6ri (hyper/meta mods missing in libghostty Mods).

title: client: urxvt-1015 decimal mouse parser
description: Niche mouse format CSI <btn>;<col>;<row>M (no leading <). Similar to SGR but without the < marker. Dispatcher already exists in input.rs; add a branch that recognises 'M' final with numeric-only params.
close_reason: Landed in commit 5f4fbc0 via PR #12. urxvt-1015 decimal mouse (CSI <btn>;<col>;<row>M, no leading <). 4 unit tests (click/release/wheel). Shipped together with phux-0wx.

title: client: legacy X10 mouse parser (CSI M Cb Cx Cy)
description: phux-19e covered SGR (DEC mode 1006) only. X10 legacy format encodes mouse reports as the literal 3 bytes after CSI M (button | 32, col + 32, row + 32). Needed for terminals that don't enable SGR mouse extensions. Implement in crates/phux-client/src/attach/input.rs (new CSI consumer-state for the 3 trailing bytes after 'M' final).
close_reason: Landed in commit 5f4fbc0 via PR #12. X10 legacy mouse (CSI M Cb Cx Cy) — new 3-byte consumer state in input.rs. 5 unit tests (click/release/shift). Shipped together with phux-jes.

title: Salvage phux-a87 E2E test scenarios from stale agent branch
description:
An agent shipped two E2E test files in commit 5c3e034 (561 LOC) on local branch worktree-agent-ad64090de85336fa1:
- crates/phux-server/tests/multi_client_scenario.rs (230 LOC)
- crates/phux-server/tests/reconnect_scenario.rs (331 LOC)

Parent epic: phux-a87 (End-to-end test harness).

Salvaged at ~95% fidelity from 5c3e034 - only API drift was TerminalId becoming a tagged enum (Local/Satellite); original tests already asserted on TERMINAL_OUTPUT via Screen oracle so ADR-0013's diff-to-bytes shift didn't bite. Both reconnect_scenario.rs (331 LOC) and multi_client_scenario.rs (231 LOC) ship green.

title: RTT-adaptive tick interval (Mosh-style, clamp [20ms, 200ms])
description:
Replace fixed 33 Hz tick (phux-q0e.C) with per-consumer RTT-adaptive interval per ADR-0018 / Mosh §3.

Measure RTT via PING/PONG timestamps (we have these per spec-anchors) or piggybacked timestamps on FRAME_ACK. Per-consumer tick interval ≈ RTT/2 clamped to [20 ms, 200 ms]. Adjust slowly (EMA, factor TBD).

Defer until phux-q0e.A/B/C/D are operational and we have real-traffic data to tune against. Fixed 33 Hz is fine for local UDS.

title: Wave D: ADR-0010 + ADR-0007 touch-ups for L1 cascade refinement
description: After Wave A lands. ADR-0010 status update: CC re-anchored as 'one consumer among several' per ADR-0017 (CC_FRONTEND capability is reclaimed; tmux CC carries no special standing). ADR-0007: generalize the SATELLITE tag from SessionId-only to applying uniformly across TerminalId / CollectionId / etc. per ADR-0015. Also subsumes phux-7vi (ADR cleanup for 0004/0006/0007/0008/0010/0012 post-ADR-0013) — fold that work in here.
close_reason: Wave D complete in commit 31a804f (was 1568070 in worktree). Update notes on ADR-0004/0006/0007/0008/0010/0012/0014. ADR-0010: tmux CC reservation overturned by ADR-0017 (CC_FRONTEND reclaimed). ADR-0007: federation tag generalized from SessionId to all identities per ADR-0015. ADR-0014 (server-terminal-pane-actor): PaneActor→TerminalActor noted. ADR-0018 verified clean (already uses TerminalId post-2026-05-26 addendum). just check + rustdoc -D warnings green.

title: phux-roz follow-up: tighten terminal reset on signal — held Termios + consumer-owned guard
description:
Today's phux-roz fix installs SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers + a panic hook that run terminal_reset_on_signal before exit. The reset is BEST-EFFORT: it re-cooks the terminal (forces ICANON + ECHO + ISIG flags on) rather than restoring the user's original Termios, because the saved Termios lives in the RawModeGuard which the signal handler can't safely reach.

Acceptable today (the user's outer shell still works after; user can 'reset' if anything sticks), but a cleaner shape:

1. Move saved Termios into a process-global Mutex<Option<Termios>> that:
   - RawModeGuard::install_with_stdout populates on entry.
   - terminal_reset_on_signal reads on signal/panic.
   - RawModeGuard::Drop reads + clears on normal unwind.
   The signal-safety of accessing a Mutex from a tokio signal arm is fine (the arm runs on the runtime thread, not in raw signal-handler context).

2. Consider whether ALT_SCREEN_ACTIVE (currently a SeqCst AtomicBool) should also live alongside, or whether the Termios's presence is itself the signal.

3. The 'libghostty renderer still writes to real stdout because its iterators aren't generic over Write' note in the wave-5 agent's design call could be revisited if a future test or alternate consumer surface needs the renderer to write into a captured buffer.

Naturally cascades with the L1 rename and the consumer-side detach policy (phux-it8 follow-up): if 'detach' becomes a consumer choice rather than a server-driven event, the consumer's RawModeGuard cleanup becomes the only place the reset story matters.

Commit reference: 63dc6ff.
close_reason: Landed on main as 00288fc (cherry-picked from fanout/phux-2r7-rawmode). Process-global SAVED_TERMIOS: Mutex<Option<Termios>> populated by RawModeGuard::install_with_stdout; signal arms + panic hook now read it to restore the user's ORIGINAL termios (not just re-cook ICANON+ECHO+ISIG). ALT_SCREEN_ACTIVE kept separate (decision documented inline) because alt-screen ENTER and termios flip happen at distinct points. 722 tests pass + 1 #[ignore]'d manual SIGINT repro.

title: phux-7vx follow-up: per-Terminal TERM as L1 SPAWN field; restore TERM=ghostty when round-trip is proven
description:
Today's phux-7vx fix swaps the default TERM=ghostty -> TERM=xterm-256color on the server-spawned PTY (pane_actor.rs:default_shell_command). Reason: TERM=ghostty's terminfo advertises 'fullkbd' which newer ncurses interprets as 'kitty keyboard protocol safe', so htop/less/vim/etc. push CSI > N u, libghostty pivots to CSI-u for every keypress, and TUIs that don't speak kitty (like htop) drop quit/F-keys.

Two follow-on tasks live under this:

1. PROVE THE ROUND-TRIP. Validate phux's input/output layer round-trips kitty KIP correctly for at least: htop, less, vim, neovim, fzf, btop. Integration tests using the wave-4 Screen harness (Screen helper at crates/phux-server/tests/common/screen.rs) where possible, manual smoke tests where not. Once this is green, restore TERM=ghostty default and the user gets ghostty's full terminfo capabilities.

2. EXPOSE TERM AS L1 SPAWN CONFIG. Under ADR-0015 the L1 SPAWN message creates a Terminal. The PTY env (TERM, LANG, PHUX_*, etc.) is part of that spawn. Today the server hardcodes TERM in default_shell_command. Move to a per-Terminal env-map field in the SPAWN request so a consumer (TUI or agent) can override it. Default value lives in phux-config (when that crate gets fleshed out). Blocked by L1 cascade for the wire-message shape.

Commit reference: 9734d42 (the current hardcoded swap).

title: phux-it8 follow-up: reshape EOF as L1 TERMINAL_CLOSED + consumer-side detach policy
description:
Today's phux-it8 fix has the server send FrameKind::Detached when a pane's PTY EOFs. Under ADR-0015's L1 substrate, the more honest shape is:
- Server emits TERMINAL_CLOSED { terminal_id, reason, exit_status } (an L1 lifecycle event)
- The CONSUMER decides what to do:
  - A TUI with no remaining terminals in its attached Collection may choose to detach itself
  - An agent SDK consumer just notes the exit code and continues subscribing/observing
  - A recorder consumer logs the close and keeps going

Currently the server bakes the TUI's 'no terminals left -> detach' policy in. Behaviorally identical for v0.1 single-pane sessions; only matters once Collections support multi-Terminal lifecycles and non-TUI consumers exist.

Implementation when this gets picked up:
- Rename FrameKind::Detached's role: detach as a state change owned by the client request, separate from terminal lifecycle.
- Add FrameKind::TerminalClosed { terminal_id, reason, exit_status } per ADR-0015 §L1 lifecycle.
- Move the 'no panes -> detach client' policy out of server runtime; into TUI consumer code (likely crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs main_loop).
- Update phux-server/tests/eof_detach.rs to assert TerminalClosed arrives; add a separate TUI-consumer test that asserts the TUI detaches in response.

Blocked by: L1 vocabulary cascade (PaneId -> TerminalId rename; see phux-byc.5 / ADR-0015 §Doc impact / ADR-0016).

Commit reference: b5b7617 (the current shape).
notes: DEFERRED 2026-05-29 (campaign triage): P3, and the ticket itself states it is behaviorally identical for v0.1 single-pane sessions — the 'consumer-owned detach policy' only matters once Collections support multi-Terminal lifecycles and non-TUI consumers exist. Moving the 'no panes -> detach' policy from server (on_terminal_exited) to the client touches the lifecycle suite (eof_detach, reconnect, server_self_exit) for zero v0.1 behavioral gain. The 'reason' field is a clean additive wire change but low value alone. Revisit alongside Collections multi-Terminal work or the q0e wiring (phux-0q8).

title: ADR cleanup: refresh 0004/0006/0007/0008/0010/0012 references to diff-based architecture (post-ADR-0013)
description:
ADR-0013 supersedes ADR-0002 (diff-based protocol). Six other ADRs reference the diff-based architecture and now read stale:

- ADR-0004 line 10: 'source of truth for diffs (ADR-0002)' wording — libghostty-vt is now source of truth for bytes forwarded and snapshots synthesized, not diffs.
- ADR-0006 line 179: brief reference, low impact.
- ADR-0007 line 36: Mosh-decomposition table row 'Cell-level state + diffs | Already done. This is the bet of ADR-0002. | phux-protocol::diff' — wrong. Update the bet to ADR-0013 and the location to 'phux-protocol::wire::frame::PaneOutput'. Recommend an ADR-0008-style 'What this ADR replaces' amendment pattern.
- ADR-0008 §1: 'what stays phux-defined' lists Cell, Grid, DiffOp, CursorState, CursorShape — most are going away. Update to reflect that wire-side definitions shrink to envelopes + PANE_OUTPUT + PANE_SNAPSHOT bytes payload.
- ADR-0010 lines 49-58, 248-249: native>CC argument leans on cell-diffs. Still wins under ADR-0013 (automatic libghostty feature parity + simpler wire + bytes-friendliness for non-libghostty consumers), but prose needs a refresh.
- ADR-0012 line 202: layout snapshots flow through diff frames — minor; the snapshot mechanism changes but layout-on-wire is still structured (lifecycle concern, not pane content).

Single docs commit. No code touched. Hashimoto-bar prose preserved.
close_reason: Subsumed by Wave D (phux-vp0.3, commit 31a804f). The ADR cleanup pass landed as part of the L1 vocabulary cascade.

title: Define MouseProtocol and MouseEncoding enums per SPEC §8.5 mode bits
description: SPEC §8.5 specifies PaneModes carries a 4-bit MOUSE_PROTOCOL field (0x00F0) and a 4-bit MOUSE_ENCODING field (0x0F00), but does not enumerate the values. phux-429 landed PaneModes with u8 accessors and a TODO; this ticket defines the enums once input plumbing demands them. Likely values: MouseProtocol = { Off, X10, Normal, ButtonEvent, AnyEvent }; MouseEncoding = { Default, Utf8, Sgr, Urxvt } per common terminal practice. Coordinate with libghostty-vt's mouse:: types — if it has equivalent enums we should re-export per ADR-0008.
